🔢 Numbered steps
1. Launch chatbot.
2. Accept disclaimer.
3. Authenticate booking.
4. Verify auto-rebooked itinerary shown.
5. Select accept.
6. Verify confirmation message.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that a customer with an eligible disrupted booking can successfully accept the automatically proposed reaccommodated itinerary.

Preconditions:
• Passenger: Tom Smith
• PNR: AC7K9L
• Original Flight: AC103 Toronto (YYZ) → Vancouver (YVR)
• Passenger already automatically rebooked to alternate itinerary

Given Tom Smith has Air Canada booking PNR AC7K9L for flight AC104 Toronto → Vancouver on 20-Jun-2026
And Tom opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed " Please note  that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot ask "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Dependingon the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to youy preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?" 
And Tom accepts the disclaimer

IDENTIFICATION:
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Tom Smith"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Tom! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR AC7K9L"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR AC7K9L
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)

AUTHENTICATION:
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: t***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: t***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."

JOURNEY SELECTION:
Then the chatbot retrieves booking details and displays:
"I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC 103
• Route: Toronto (YYZ) → Vancouver (YVR)
• Date: 20-Jun-2026
Is this the journey you'd like to check?"
And the user confirms "Yes"

And the system validates booking eligibility successfully
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journye hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."
Then the chatbot checks if the passenger have a new rebooking → Yes
Then the chatbot responds: "Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight."
And new itinerary comparison is displayed with Old Flight card And New Flight card
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"
And chatbot displays three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And the user selects Confirm proposed itinerary
Then chatbot responds: "Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly."
Then the chatbot responds: "Here' an overview of your new booking:
And the New itinerary summary is displayed

END FLOW:
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
Customer successfully accepts proposed itinerary. Booking confirmed.

📋 Raw Action / Description

📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that customer can search and select alternate dates within airline-approved waiver window (±3 days).

Preconditions:
• Passenger: Michael Brown
• PNR: DT8M4Q
• Flight: AC306 Ottawa (YOW) → Halifax (YHZ)

Given Michael Brown has Air Canada booking PNR DT8M4Q for flight AC312 Ottawa(YOW) →  Halifax(YHZ) on 20-Jun-2026
And Michael opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed " Please note  that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot ask "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Dependingon the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to youy preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?" 
And Michael accepts the disclaimer

IDENTIFICATION:
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Michael Brown"

"Thanks, Michael! And, what is your booking reference number?"

And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)

AUTHENTICATION:
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: d***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: d***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."

JOURNEY SELECTION:
Then the chatbot retrieves booking details and displays:
"I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC 312
• Route: Ottawa (YOW) → Halifax (YHZ)
• Date: 20-Jun-2026
Is this the journey you'd like to check?"
And the user confirms "Yes"

And the system validates booking eligibility successfully
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."
Then the chatbot checks if the passenger have a new rebooking → Yes
Then the chatbot responds: "Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight."
And new itinerary comparison is displayed with Old Flight card And New Flight card
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"
And chatbot displays three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And the user selects "Select a new flight"
Then the chatbot checks if the user is self-serve eligible based on baggage → Yes
Then the chatbot checks if the user have SSRs → No

ALTERNATE FLIGHT SELECTION (INVOL):
Then the chatbot responds: "Let's look into some alternative flights together. Pleasescroll and select your preferred new itinerary."
And the chatbot displays the flight options (Top 5 to start)
And the user responds: "I don't like any of these options."
And the chatbot responds: "Can you give me more information for when you want to be rebooked? How would you like to proceed?"
And the user mentions: "I want a flight for June 22, 2026"
And the chatbot checks if the date is ±3 days from the original date → Yes
Then the chatbot responds: "Let's look into some alternative flights together. Pleasescroll and select your preferred new itinerary."
And the chatbot displays the flight options (Top 5 to start)
And the chatbot responds: "Got it! Here's a suummary of the requested change:"
And the chatbot displays the new itinerary comparsion 
And the user confirms the change
And the chatbot asks: "Would you like to proceed?"
And the user responds: "Yes"
And the chatbot responds: "Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly."
And the chatbot mentions: "Here's an overview of your new booking:"
And the chatbot displays the new itinerary summary 

END FLOW:
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Please have access to youy preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?" 
And Sarah accepts the disclaimer

IDENTIFICATION:
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Sarah Mitchell"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Sarah ! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR SM4R8K"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR SM4R8K
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)

AUTHENTICATION:
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: s***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: s***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."

JOURNEY SELECTION:
Then the chatbot retrieves booking details and displays:
"I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC 312
• Route: Ottawa (YOW) → Halifax (YHZ)
• Date: 20-Jun-2026
Is this the journey you'd like to check?"
And the user confirms "Yes"

And the system validates booking eligibility successfully
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."
Then the chatbot checks if the passenger have a new rebooking → Yes
Then the chatbot responds: "Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight."
And new itinerary comparison is displayed with Old Flight card And New Flight card
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"
And chatbot displays three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And the user selects "Confirm Proposed itinerary"
Then the chatbot responds: "Your ticket has been re-issued. You should recieve an email confirmation shortly."

END FLOW:
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Acknowledgment successful.

🔢 Numbered steps
1. Launch chatbot and authenticate (PNR NP8T4Q + Moore).
2. Verify no rebooking exists (No-Pro).
3. Validate 2 options displayed (Change, Refund).
4. Select Change flight.
5. Verify self-serve checks (SSR, baggage, etc.).
6. Verify Alternative Search API call.
7. Validate flight options displayed.
8. Select AC710.
9. Confirm offer.10. Verify Confirm Alternate Offer API.
11. Validate PNR update (AC700 → AC710).
12. Validate ticket reissue.
13. Verify confirmation, email, and termination.
📋 Raw Action / Description

"I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC778

Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journye hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."

And there is a delay of 18 minutes
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"
And chatbot displays three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Cancel my booking
And the user selects Confirm proposed itinerary

And the New itinerary summary is displayed

And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

• Email: s***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: s***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."

Then the chatbot retrieves booking details and displays:
"I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC 145
• Route: Calgary (YYC) → Toronto (YYZ)
• Date: 20-Jun-2026
Is this the journey you'd like to check?"
And the user confirms "Yes"
And the system validates booking eligibility successfully
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."
Then the chatbot checks if the passenger have a new rebooking → Yes
Then the chatbot responds: "Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight."
And new itinerary comparison is displayed with Old Flight card And New Flight card
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"
And chatbot displays three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And the user selects "Select a new flight"
Then the chatbot checks if the user is self-serve eligible based on baggage → Yes (checked baggage exists but does not block — domestic non-mid-journey)
Then the chatbot checks if the user have SSRs → No
Then the chatbot checks if the user have special meals → No
ALTERNATE FLIGHT SELECTION (INVOL):
Then the chatbot responds: "Let's look into some alternative flights together. Please scroll and select your preferred new itinerary."
And the chatbot displays the flight options (Top 5 to start)
And the user selects flight AC152
And the chatbot responds: "Got it! Here's a summary of the requested change:"
And the chatbot displays the new itinerary comparison
And the user confirms the change
And the chatbot asks: "Would you like to proceed?"
And the user responds: "Yes"
And Confirm Alternate Offer API succeeds
And Ticket Reissue API completes successfully
And the chatbot responds: "Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly."
And the chatbot mentions: "Here's an overview of your new booking:"
And the chatbot displays the new itinerary summary
CHECKED BAGGAGE NOTIFICATION:
Then the chatbot displays:
"Your checked baggage will continue to your final destination."
And confirmation email is sent to s***@gmail.com
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Rebooking completed successfully. Baggage transfer message shown. Confirmation sent.

• Outbound Flight: AC847 Toronto (YYZ) → Frankfurt (FRA) – Cancelled
• Return Flight: AC848 Frankfurt (FRA) → Toronto (YYZ) – Active

📋 Raw Action / Description

• PNR: SM4R8K
• Customer already acknowledged itinerary

🔢 Numbered steps
Launch chatbot on mobile device at YUL airport.
Verify Service Error API returns no outage.
Accept legal disclaimer.
Select "My flight has been cancelled."
Enter PNR "MJ8K5R" and surname "Chen."
Verify Authentication API retrieves booking successfully.
Complete OTP verification.
Verify Order Retrieve API identifies:
Segment 1: AC401 YYZ → YUL = FLOWN
Segment 2: AC870 YUL → LHR = UN (cancelled)
Verify system detects mid-journey state — passenger has flown Segment 1 but not reached final destination (LHR).
Verify EIP isDisrupted = true for the disrupted bound.
Verify elapsed time since disruption > 30 minutes — no wait advisory.
Verify booking source "ACB" validated as eligible.
Verify SSR scan clean — no blocking SSRs.
Verify Mid-Journey Baggage Rules service is called.
Verify service detects 1 checked bag tagged to LHR (international, non-Canadian, non-USA).
Verify booking overview displays:
Segment 1: AC401 YYZ → YUL — FLOWN
Segment 2: AC870 YUL → LHR — CANCELLED
Verify two options displayed (No-Pro): "Select a new flight" | "Cancel my booking."
Select "Select a new flight."
Verify System performs Self-Serve Checks Check 1 (checked baggage) triggered.
Verify checked bag detected on booking.
Verify chatbot displays ADD-60 verbatim: "I'm sorry, since you have a checked bag on your booking, you'll need to speak to an Air Canada baggage agent at the airport."
Verify System performs Self-Serve Checks Check 2 and Check 3 are NOT reached.
Verify BCInvol-05 is NOT reached.
Verify NO Live Agent Hand-off Live Agent Handoff triggered.
Verify flow terminates (End Flow).
Verify no PNR modifications committed. No ticket reissued.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate the complete E2E flow when a passenger is mid-journey (first segment already flown), their connecting segment is involuntarily cancelled, and they have checked baggage.
Preconditions: • Passenger: Laura Chen • PNR: MJ8K5R • Itinerary: 2-segment connecting journey — Segment 1: AC401 Toronto (YYZ) → Montreal (YUL) — FLOWN (past date, boarded) — Segment 2: AC870 Montreal (YUL) → London Heathrow (LHR) — CANCELLED (UN) • Checked baggage: 1 bag checked through to LHR (international destination) • Booking source: ACB (Air Canada Direct) • Ticket stock: 014 • No blocking SSRs (no PETC, UPGD, GRPS, etc.) • No special meals • Laura is currently at YUL airport (mid-journey) • Disruption occurred more than 30 minutes ago • disruptionStatus.isDisrupted = true

Given Laura Chen has Air Canada booking PNR MJ8K5R with a 2-segment connecting journey
And Segment 1: AC401 Toronto (YYZ) → Montreal (YUL) — FLOWN (past date, boarded)
And Segment 2: AC870 Montreal (YUL) → London Heathrow (LHR) — CANCELLED (UN)
And Laura has already completed Segment 1 (YYZ → YUL)
And Laura is currently at Montreal (YUL) airport — mid-journey state
And 1 checked bag exists, tagged through to LHR (international destination)
And booking source is ACB, ticket stock 014
And no blocking SSRs exist on the PNR (no PETC, UPGD, GRPS, etc.)
And no special meals exist on the PNR
And disruption occurred more than 30 minutes ago
And Laura opens the Air Canada chatbot on her mobile device at YUL airport
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Laura accepts the disclaimer

Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Laura Chen"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Laura! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR MJ8K5R"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR MJ8K5R
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: l***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: l***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
MID-JOURNEY DETECTION & DISRUPTION EVALUATION:
Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for PNR "MJ8K5R"
And the system retrieves 2 connecting segments:
Segment 1: AC401 YYZ → YUL — Status: FLOWN (past date, boarded)
Segment 2: AC870 YUL → LHR — Status: UN (cancelled)
And the system determines this is a mid-journey disruption
(passenger has boarded at least one segment but has not reached final destination LHR)
And EIP isDisrupted = true for the disrupted bound
And disruption type = Involuntary, changeIdentifier = "Flight_Rebooking" or "NoPro"
ELIGIBILITY EVALUATION:
And the system validates elapsed time since disruption > 30 minutes — no wait advisory triggered
And the system calls DBaaS to validate booking source — ACB = eligible
And the system scans SSRs — no blocking SSRs found (no PETC, AVIH, UMNR, UPGD, GRPS)
And the system evaluates PNR eligibility — pnrEligible = true
And the system identifies no auto-reprotection (No-Pro mid-journey)
MID-JOURNEY BAGGAGE RULES CHECK:
And the system calls Mid-Journey Baggage Rules service
And the service evaluates:
Is any bag checked to USA? → No
Is any bag checked to non-Canadian/non-USA (international)? → Yes — LHR
And the service returns: mid-journey with international checked bag
BOOKING OVERVIEW & OPTIONS (No-Pro):
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."
Then the chatbot checks if the passenger have a new rebooking → No (No-Pro — no auto-reprotection)
Then the chatbot responds: "You haven't been rebooked on a new flight yet."
And the chatbot displays the booking overview:
Segment 1: AC401 YYZ → YUL — FLOWN (completed)
Segment 2: AC870 YUL → LHR — CANCELLED (UN)
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"
And chatbot displays two options (No-Pro — no rebooked flight to confirm):
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And Laura selects "Select a new flight"
SELF-SERVE CHECKS — CHECK 1 (CHECKED BAGGAGE):
Then System performs Self-Serve Checks Check 1 (checked baggage) is triggered
And the system detects checked bag on the booking tagged to LHR (international destination)
CHECKED BAGGAGE — MID-JOURNEY BLOCK:
Then chatbot shall display ADD-60 baggage verbatim:
"I'm sorry, since you have a checked bag on your booking, you'll need to speak to an Air Canada baggage agent at the airport."
And System performs Self-Serve Checks Check 2 (SSR/Pet) shall NOT be reached
And System performs Self-Serve Checks Check 3 (special meal) shall NOT be reached
And BCInvol-05 (Alt Flight Selection) shall NOT be reached
And NO Live Agent Handoff shall be triggered (customer must speak to airport agent, not online agent)
And the flow shall terminate (End Flow)
And no booking changes shall be committed
And no ticket reissue shall be triggered
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
Authentication and disruption validation succeed.

Mid-journey state correctly identified: Segment 1 flown (YYZ → YUL), Segment 2 cancelled (YUL → LHR).

Mid-Journey Baggage Rules service confirms checked bag to international destination (LHR).

Booking overview correctly displays flown and cancelled segments.

Two options shown (No-Pro — no rebooked flight to confirm).

Customer selects "Select a new flight."

System performs Self-Serve Checks Check 1 (baggage) blocks self-service immediately.

Message displayed: "I'm sorry, since you have a checked bag on your booking, you'll need to speak to an Air Canada baggage agent at the airport."
End Flow — NOT Live Agent Hand-off Live Agent Handoff (airport agent, not online agent).

No booking changes. No ticket reissue. Customer must speak to airport baggage agent.

📋 Raw Action / Description
Description:
1. Disrupted AC Direct passenger with TK status completes seat change.

Preconditions:
1. Original AC870 cancelled, rebooked on AC872
2. Source = "AC Direct", segment = "TK"

Given the passenger has Air Canada booking with original flight AC870 that was cancelled
And Air Canada has auto-rebooked the passenger on flight AC872
And booking source = "AC Direct"
And segment status = "TK" (ticket confirmed)
And the passenger opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And the passenger accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides their full name
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, [First Name]! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides their PNR
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for the PNR
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: [masked email]
• Phone: [masked phone]
And the user selects Email
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
JOURNEY SELECTION:
Then the chatbot retrieves booking details and displays:
"I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC 870
• Route: [Origin] → [Destination]
• Date: [Date]
Is this the journey you'd like to check?"
And the user confirms "Yes"
And the system validates booking eligibility successfully
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."
Then the chatbot checks if the passenger have a new rebooking → Yes
Then the chatbot responds: "Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight."
And new itinerary comparison is displayed with Old Flight card (AC870 — Cancelled) And New Flight card (AC872 — Confirmed)
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"
And chatbot displays three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And the user selects "Confirm proposed itinerary"
SELF-SERVE CHECKS:
Then System performs Self-Serve Checks — baggage check passes (no checked baggage)
And System performs Self-Serve Checks — SSR/Pet check passes (no blocking SSRs)
And System performs Self-Serve Checks — special meal check passes (no special meals)
SEAT CHANGE ELIGIBILITY:
Then the chatbot displays:
"You are eligible to change your seat for this rebooked flight."
And the chatbot responds: "Would you like to select a seat for your rebooked flight AC872?"
And the user selects "Yes"
SEAT CHANGE FLOW (SC-01 → SC-02 → SC-03):
Then the chatbot checks which airline → AC (AC family carrier)
Then the chatbot responds: "I can help you add your seat."
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that there may be a fee based on your selection."
And a Seat Change-MODAL is being displayed with the aircraft seat layout for AC872
SC-02 — SEAT SELECTION:
When the user browses the seat map
And the user selects a preferred seat from the layout
And clicks on "Confirm" button
Then the seat selection is recorded
SC-03 — CHARGES DISPLAY:
Then the chatbot displays the seat change charges:
"Here's a breakdown of the charges for your seat selection:
Seat: [Selected Seat Number]
Seat Fee: CAD $[amount]
Would you like to proceed with payment?"
And the user confirms "Yes"
PAYMENT:
Then the chatbot generates payment link inside the active session
And the user clicks the payment link
And the user is redirected to the external payment page
And the user completes payment of CAD $[amount] successfully
And the user returns to the chatbot
SC-03a — CONFIRMATION:
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your seat has been confirmed and your ticket has been updated. You should receive an email confirmation shortly."
And the chatbot displays:
"Here's an overview of your updated booking:
Flight: AC872
Route: [Origin] → [Destination]
Date: [Date]
Seat: [Selected Seat Number]
A confirmation email has been sent to [masked email]."

Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

🔢 Numbered steps
Setup: Create ACB booking with 2 passengers. Cancel flight. Split PNR — Diana moved to child PNR AB6M9Q.
Launch chatbot as Diana.
Accept legal disclaimer.
Enter child PNR "AB6M9Q" and surname "Hall."
Complete OTP verification.
Verify booking source on child PNR = ACB (retained from parent).
Verify self-service is eligible.
Verify booking overview displayed: AC450 (cancelled) and AC455 (rebooked).
Verify three involuntary options displayed (disruption exists).
Select "Select a new flight."
Verify baggage check passes — no bags.
Verify SSR/Pet check passes — no blocking SSRs.
Verify special meal check passes — no meals.
Select date and alternate flight AC460.
Confirm selection.
Verify Confirm Alternate Offer API succeeds.
Verify booking updated to AC460 at no cost.
Verify ticket reissued and confirmation displayed.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Contrast case: validate that when an ACB (Air Canada Direct) booking is split, the child PNR retains ACB source and remains eligible for self-service. 
Preconditions: • Passengers: Chris Hall (ADT), Diana Hall (ADT) • Original PNR: AB6M9P (ACB booking source) • Original Flight: AC450 YUL → YOW — CANCELLED (UN), rebooked AC455 (HK) • PNR split at airport — Diana moved to child PNR: AB6M9Q • Diana contacts chatbot

PHASE 1 — SETUP (PRE-TEST):
Given passengers Chris Hall and Diana Hall had booking PNR "AB6M9P"
And booking source = ACB (Air Canada Direct)
And original flight AC450 YUL → YOW has been cancelled (UN)
And Diana auto-rebooked on AC455 (HK)
And PNR was split at airport:
Parent PNR: AB6M9P — Chris Hall — ACB source retained
Child PNR: AB6M9Q — Diana Hall — Source = ACB (retained from parent)

PHASE 2 — CHATBOT ENTRY:
When Diana opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And Diana responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Diana accepts the disclaimer

IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Diana Hall"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Diana! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR AB6M9Q"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR AB6M9Q
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: d***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: d***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
BOOKING SOURCE EVALUATION:
Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for child PNR "AB6M9Q"
And the system evaluates booking source
And the system identifies booking source as ACB (Air Canada Direct — retained from parent PNR after split)
And ACB bookings ARE eligible for INVOL self-service
ELIGIBILITY EVALUATION:
Then BCINVOL eligibility returns ELIGIBLE
And no blocking SSRs detected
And no checked baggage blocking
And no special meals blocking

"I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC 450
• Route: Montreal (YUL) → Ottawa (YOW)
• Date: [Date]
Is this the journey you'd like to check?"
And the user confirms "Yes"
And the system validates booking eligibility successfully
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."
Then the chatbot checks if the passenger have a new rebooking → Yes
Then the chatbot responds: "Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight."
And new itinerary comparison is displayed with Old Flight card (AC450 — Cancelled) And New Flight card (AC455 — Confirmed)
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"
And chatbot displays three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And Diana selects "Select a new flight"
SELF-SERVE CHECKS:
Then the chatbot checks if the user is self-serve eligible based on baggage → Yes (no checked baggage)
Then the chatbot checks if the user have SSRs → No (no blocking SSRs)
Then the chatbot checks if the user have special meals → No (no special meals)
ALTERNATE FLIGHT SELECTION (INVOL):
Then the chatbot responds: "Let's look into some alternative flights together. Please scroll and select your preferred new itinerary."

Expected Outcome:
Child PNR retains ACB source → self-service eligible.
Contrast with SplitPNR_Source_TC001 (ACV retained → blocked).
Full self-service flow completes successfully on split child PNR.
Confirms booking source persistence logic works correctly for both eligible and ineligible sources.

💬 Customer intent from test case (narrative)
👤 Already-rebooked flight excluded from alternate flight search results Passenger have already been rebooked on a particular flight, it shouldn't be visible on the alternate flight options again. capturing the alternate flight options that are visible should not be the same as, like should not include one that's already been rebooked to.

🔢 Numbered steps
1.Launch chatbot and accept legal disclaimer.
2.Select "My flight has been cancelled."
3.Enter PNR "SM4R8K" and surname "Mitchell."
4.Verify booking retrieved, disruption confirmed, eligibility passes.
5.Verify booking overview shows AC104 (cancelled) and AC118 (rebooked).
6.Verify three buttons displayed: "Confirm proposed itinerary" | "Select a new flight" | "Cancel my booking."
7.Select "Select a new flight."
8.Verify System performs Self-Serve Checks checks pass — chatbot proceeds to Date Selection & Flight Search.
9.Select departure date within ±3 day window.
10.Verify Flight Search API returns alternate flights.
11.Verify AC118 (already-rebooked flight) is NOT present in search results.
12.Verify all displayed flights are different from AC118.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that when a rebooked passenger rejects the proposed itinerary and searches for alternates, the flight they are already rebooked on does NOT appear in the alternate search results. Preconditions: • Passenger: Sarah Mitchell • PNR: SM4R8K • Original Flight: AC104 YYZ → YVR — Cancelled (UN) • Auto-rebooked on AC118 YYZ → YVR — Confirmed (HK) • isRebooked = true, change.isEligible = true • Booking source: ACB, no ineligible SSRs, no checked baggage, no special meals

# ── PRECONDITIONS ──

Given passenger Sarah Mitchell has booking PNR "SM4R8K"
And original flight AC104 is cancelled (UN)
And Sarah is auto-rebooked on AC118 (HK)
And isRebooked = true and change.isEligible = true
And booking source is ACB, no blocking SSRs, no baggage, no special meals

# ── TEST STEPS ──

When Sarah launches chatbot and accepts the legal disclaimer
And selects "My flight has been cancelled"
And enters PNR "SM4R8K" and surname "Mitchell"
And Authentication API retrieves booking successfully
And the system validates disruption, rebooking, and eligibility — pnrEligible = true
And the chatbot displays booking overview with AC104 (cancelled) and AC118 (rebooked)
And the chatbot displays three options: "Confirm proposed itinerary" | "Select a new flight" | "Cancel my booking"
And Sarah selects "Select a new flight"
And System performs Self-Serve Checks checks pass (no baggage, no SSR blocks, no special meals)
And chatbot proceeds to Date Selection & Flight Search with date selection within ±3 days
And Sarah selects the original departure date
And Alternative Flight Search API returns available flights

# ── EXPECTED RESULTS ──

Then alternate flight results shall be displayed
And flight AC118 (already-rebooked flight) shall NOT appear in the results
And every flight displayed shall be a different flight from AC118
And only genuinely new alternate options shall be visible to the customer

Expected Outcome:
Authentication, disruption, and eligibility validations pass.

Booking overview correctly shows AC104 (cancelled) and AC118 (rebooked).

System performs Self-Serve Checks checks pass. Date Selection & Flight Search reached.

AC118 is correctly EXCLUDED from alternate flight results.

No duplicate of the rebooked itinerary appears in search results.

Customer sees only genuinely new flight options.

📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that customer cannot proceed if the mandatory legal disclaimer is not accepted.

Preconditions:
• Passenger has launched Air Canada chatbot disruption flow

Given a customer opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
LEGAL DISCLAIMER:
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And the chatbot displays two options:
• I agree
• I do not agree
When the customer selects "I do not agree"
FLOW TERMINATED:
Then the chatbot shall terminate the disruption support flow
And no booking authentication screen shall be displayed
And no identification questions shall be asked
And no PNR entry field shall be shown
And no OTP verification shall occur
And the chatbot does NOT proceed to any BCInvol self-service step
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
Flow terminated. Customer cannot continue.

📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that multiple failed booking authentication attempts are blocked for security purposes.

Preconditions:
• No valid booking exists

Given a customer opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And the customer accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "John Brown"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, John! And, what is your booking reference number?"
ATTEMPT 1:
And the user provides "PNR ZZZZZZ"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR ZZZZZZ
And Authentication API returns: booking not found
And the chatbot responds: "Invalid Credentials Entered, Please retry again. Note: You will have maximum 2 more attempts to retry"
ATTEMPT 2:
And the user provides "PNR ZZZZZZ" again
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR ZZZZZZ
And Authentication API returns: booking not found
And the chatbot responds: "Invalid Credentials Entered, Please retry again. Note: You will have maximum 1 more attempt to retry"
ATTEMPT 3:
And the user provides "PNR ZZZZZZ" again
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR ZZZZZZ
And Authentication API returns: booking not found
AUTHENTICATION BLOCKED:
Then the chatbot shall display:
"We're unable to verify your booking at this time."
And further attempts shall be blocked
And no additional PNR entry field shall be displayed
And the chatbot shall NOT proceed to OTP verification
And the chatbot shall NOT proceed to any BCInvol self-service step
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
Authentication blocked after retry threshold.

📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate surname mismatch handling during booking authentication.

Preconditions:
• Passenger booking exists

Given a customer opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And the customer accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Jane Doe" (incorrect surname — actual passenger is a different person)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Jane! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides a valid PNR
SURNAME MISMATCH:
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for the provided PNR
And Authentication API validates the PNR — PNR exists
And Authentication API validates surname "Doe" against the PNR passenger list
And Authentication API detects surname mismatch — "Doe" does not match any passenger on the PNR
Then the chatbot shall display authentication error message:
"Invalid Credentials Entered, Please retry again. Note: You will have maximum 2 more attempts to retry"
And retry shall be allowed
And the user provides the correct surname on the next attempt
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer again
And Authentication API validates surname match → SUCCESS
And the chatbot proceeds to OTP authentication
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the standard OTP flow continues normally

Expected Outcome:
Authentication error shown. Retry permitted.

⚙️ Eligibility API returns selfReac = false with a reason code.
🤖
We have to tell them why they're not eligible. We can't just say you're not eligible.
🔢 Numbered steps
1.Launch chatbot and accept legal disclaimer.
2.Select "My flight has been cancelled."
3.Enter PNR "NR5K8L" and surname "Smith."
4.Verify Authentication API retrieves booking successfully.
5.Complete OTP verification (if prompted).
6.Verify Order Retrieve API confirms disruption — AC210 = UN, isDisrupted = true.
7.Verify Eligibility API is called and returns selfReac = false with reason code.
8.Verify chatbot displays reason-specific ineligibility message (not a generic "not eligible").
9.Verify no self-service options are displayed (no confirm, no search, no refund buttons).
10.Verify flow terminates or routes to support based on reason code.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that customers whose booking is not eligible for self-reaccommodation are blocked from BCInvol flow.

Preconditions:
• Passenger: Tom Smith
• PNR: NR5K8L
• selfReac = false

Given Tom Smith has Air Canada booking PNR NR5K8L for flight AC210 Toronto → Ottawa on 20-Jun-2026
And the flight has been cancelled (segment status = UN)
And booking source is ACB
And disruption occurred more than 30 minutes ago
And Eligibility API will return selfReac = false
And Tom opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Tom accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Tom Smith"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Tom! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR NR5K8L"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR NR5K8L
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: t***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: t***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
JOURNEY SELECTION:
Then the chatbot retrieves booking details and displays:
"I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC 210
• Route: Toronto (YYZ) → Ottawa (YOW)
• Date: 20-Jun-2026
Is this the journey you'd like to check?"
And the user confirms "Yes"
ELIGIBILITY EVALUATION:
Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for PNR "NR5K8L"
And the system confirms disruption — AC210 = UN, isDisrupted = true
And the system calls Eligibility API to evaluate selfReac flag
And Eligibility API returns selfReac = false with a reason code
SELF-REAC RESTRICTION:
Then the chatbot shall block the self-service BCInvol flow
And the chatbot shall display a reason-specific ineligibility message explaining why the booking cannot be changed through self-service
And the customer shall NOT see a generic "not eligible" message
And no alternate flight search options shall be displayed
And no "Confirm proposed itinerary" button shall be shown
And no "Select a new flight" button shall be shown
And no "Cancel my booking" button shall be shown
And the flow shall terminate or route to appropriate support based on reason code
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
Authentication and disruption validation succeed.

Eligibility API returns selfReac = false with a reason code.

Chatbot displays a reason-specific message explaining why self-service is blocked (customer must understand the reason — per transcript: "We have to tell them why they're not eligible. We can't just say you're not eligible.").

No BCInvol self-service options shown.

Flow terminates or routes to appropriate support channel.

📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that passengers automatically rebooked onto Other Airline (OAL) carriers cannot modify their itinerary through BCInvol self-service.

Preconditions:
• Passenger: James Peterson
• PNR: LM4R8Q
• Rebooked on Lufthansa LH2231

Given James Peterson has Air Canada booking PNR LM4R8Q for a disrupted flight on 20-Jun-2026
And James has been automatically rebooked onto a partner airline flight (Lufthansa LH2231)
And James opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And James accepts the disclaimer

Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:

And the user provides "James Peterson"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, James! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR LM4R8Q"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR LM4R8Q

• Email: j***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: j***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
OAL VALIDATION:
Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for PNR "LM4R8Q"
And OAL validation service detects partner carrier segment (Lufthansa LH2231)
And the booking contains OAL reprotection — not eligible for Air Canada self-service
OAL RESTRICTION:
Then the chatbot shall display message:
"This booking was through a third party booking platform. In order to make changes to your booking, please contact your original booking source"
And self-service BCInvol flow shall not proceed
And no "Confirm proposed itinerary" option is displayed
And no "Select a new flight" option is displayed
And no "Cancel my booking" option is displayed

"Is there anything I can help you with?"

🔢 Numbered steps
Launch chatbot and accept legal disclaimer.
Select "My flight has been cancelled."
Enter PNR "AP6K3R" and surname "Moore."
Verify Authentication API retrieves booking successfully.
Trigger API timeout / service downtime during eligibility check.
Verify chatbot displays first retry message: "It looks like we had a small disruption on our end. Can you please try again?"
Verify system retries automatically.
Trigger second API timeout / failure.
Verify chatbot displays final error message: "I'm sorry, looks like we still have technical problems. Unfortunately, your progress was not saved. Please try again later. We are sorry for the inconvenience."
Verify NO live agent handoff is triggered.
Verify no booking changes or partial updates are committed.
Verify flow terminates

And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
API TIMEOUT — ATTEMPT 1:
Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for PNR "AP6K3R"
And the system encounters API timeout during eligibility check
Then the chatbot shall display first retry message:
"It looks like we had a small disruption on our end. Can you please try again?"
API TIMEOUT — ATTEMPT 2 (AUTOMATIC RETRY):
When the system retries automatically
And the retry also fails (second timeout)
Then the chatbot shall display final error message:
"I'm sorry, looks like we still have technical problems. Unfortunately, your progress was not saved. Please try again later. We are sorry for the inconvenience."
And NO live agent handoff shall be triggered
And NO booking changes shall be committed
And the flow shall terminate

Expected Outcome:
Timeout handled.
First API failure → retry message displayed, system retries automatically.

Second API failure → final error message displayed with correct "I'm sorry, looks like we still have technical problems.
   Unfortunately, your progress was not saved. Please try
   again later. We are sorry for the inconvenience.".

NO live agent handoff

No partial booking updates saved.

Flow terminates

• Passenger: Emma Roberts

• Checked baggage exists

And mid-journey bag flow is NOT active (current behavior)

Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."

And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"

• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And Emma selects "Select a new flight"

And system detects checked baggage on the booking

And chatbot shall NOT proceed to System performs Self-Serve Checks Check 3 (special meal)
And chatbot shall NOT proceed to alternate flight search

And the flow shall terminate
And no booking changes shall be committed

• No

System performs Self-Serve Checks Check 1 detects checked baggage → blocks self-service immediately.

Verbatim message displayed: "I'm sorry, since you have a checked bag on your booking, you'll need to speak to an Air Canada baggage agent at the airport."

Remaining System performs Self-Serve Checks checks (SSR/Pet, special meal) are NOT executed — baggage check is the first gate.

Flow terminates  End Flow — NOT Live Agent Hand-off Live Agent Handoff (customer must speak to airport agent, not online agent).

No booking changes committed.

And chatbot displays three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And Daniel selects "Select a new flight"
SELF-SERVE CHECKS — CHECK 1 (BAGGAGE):
Then the chatbot checks if the user is self-serve eligible based on baggage → Yes (no checked baggage)
SELF-SERVE CHECKS — CHECK 2 (SSR/MEAL/PET):
Then System performs Self-Serve Checks SSR/Meal/Pet check is triggered
And system detects PETC SSR on the booking
And PETC SSR shall block self-service
PETC RESTRICTION:
Then the chatbot displays:
"I can help you initiate a booking change. To better assist you, we'll need to rebook the flight manually and re-append your add-on services. Let me transfer you to an agent who can help you. If you're already at the airport, please speak with an Air Canada representative."
And chatbot shall NOT proceed to Date Selection & Flight Search (alternate flight search)
And Live Agent Handoff shall be triggered
And agent routing options shall be displayed
And flow terminates

Expected Outcome:
PETC SSR correctly detected at System performs Self-Serve Checks check.

Verbatim message displayed: "I can help you initiate a booking change. To better assist you, we'll need to rebook the flight manually and re-append your add-on services. Let me transfer you to an agent who can help you. If you're already at the airport, please speak with an Air Canada representative."

Self-service blocked. Alternate flight search NOT reached.

Live Agent Hand-off Live Agent Handoff initiated successfully.

Description: Validate that bookings containing upgrades cannot be serviced in BCInvol self-service flow.

Preconditions:
• Passenger: Rebecca Jones
• PNR: UG3X8W
• Upgrade exists

Given Rebecca Jones has Air Canada booking PNR UG3X8W for flight AC702 Toronto → New York on 20-Jun-2026
And the flight has been cancelled (segment status = UN)
And Rebecca is auto-rebooked (segment status = HK)
And PNR contains upgrade SSR (UPGD/UPGO)
And booking source is ACB
And no checked baggage exists
And Rebecca opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Rebecca accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Rebecca Jones"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Rebecca! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR UG3X8W"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR UG3X8W
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)

Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: r***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: r***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
JOURNEY SELECTION:
Then the chatbot retrieves booking details and displays:
"I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC 702
• Route: Toronto (YYZ) → New York (JFK)
• Date: 20-Jun-2026
Is this the journey you'd like to check?"
And the user confirms "Yes"
And the system validates booking eligibility successfully
And BCINVOL eligibility returns ELIGIBLE
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."
Then the chatbot checks if the passenger have a new rebooking → Yes
Then the chatbot responds: "Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight."
And new itinerary comparison is displayed with Old Flight card (AC702 — Cancelled) And New Flight card (rebooked flight — Confirmed)
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"
And chatbot displays three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And Rebecca selects "Select a new flight"
SELF-SERVE CHECKS — CHECK 1 (BAGGAGE):
Then the chatbot checks if the user is self-serve eligible based on baggage → Yes (no checked baggage)
SELF-SERVE CHECKS — CHECK 2 (SSR/MEAL/PET):
Then System performs Self-Serve Checks SSR/Meal/Pet check is triggered
And system detects UPGD/UPGO SSR on the booking
And UPGD/UPGO SSR is on the Rule 5 ineligible list
And UPGD/UPGO SSR shall block self-service
UPGRADE SSR RESTRICTION:
Then the chatbot displays:
"I can help you initiate a booking change. To better assist you, we'll need to rebook the flight manually and re-append your add-on services. Let me transfer you to an agent who can help you. If you're already at the airport, please speak with an Air Canada representative."
And chatbot shall NOT proceed to Date Selection & Flight Search (alternate flight search)
And Live Agent Handoff shall be triggered
And agent routing options shall be displayed
And flow terminates

🔢 Numbered steps
Open the Air Canada chatbot on Web / Mobile App / WhatsApp / iMessage.
Verify the chatbot session is active and ready for input.
Send a message related to Air Canada Vacations (e.g., "I need help with my Air Canada Vacations package").
Verify the chatbot processes the request and identifies ACV context.
Verify the chatbot displays: "For Air Canada Vacations-related requests, please contact our travel experts using the phone number listed on the Air Canada Vacations Contact Us page."
Verify a clickable link labeled "Contact Us | Travel Info & Support" is displayed.
Click the link and verify it redirects to the correct ACV Contact Us page.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validates that when a user sends a message related to an Air Canada Vacations booking, the chatbot immediately redirects them to the ACV Contact Us page with a clickable link, skips booking relation and identification questions, and proceeds to the end-of-chat flow. Preconditions:
• User has active chatbot session on Web/Mobile/WhatsApp/iMessage
• User's request context is related to ACV

Given the user opens the Air Canada chatbot on Web or Mobile App
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user sends a message related to an Air Canada Vacations booking (e.g., "I need help with my Air Canada Vacations package")
ACV INTENT DETECTION:
When the chatbot processes the request
And the chatbot identifies ACV (Air Canada Vacations) context from the user's message
ACV REDIRECT — IMMEDIATE:
Then the chatbot displays:
"For Air Canada Vacations-related requests, please contact our travel experts using the phone number listed on the Air Canada Vacations Contact Us page."
And a clickable link labeled "Contact Us | Travel Info & Support" is displayed
And the chatbot shall NOT proceed to booking authentication
And the chatbot shall NOT ask for PNR or surname
And the chatbot shall NOT display legal disclaimer for BCInvol
And no identification questions shall be asked
And no OTP verification shall occur
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
Chatbot immediately identifies the ACV-related context from the user's message.

ACV contact message is displayed with correct verbatim.

Clickable "Contact Us | Travel Info & Support" link is displayed and functional — redirects to ACV contact page.

And the user sends a message related to Air Canada Cargo (e.g., "I need help with my Air Canada Cargo shipment")
CARGO INTENT DETECTION:
When the chatbot processes the request
And the chatbot identifies AC Cargo context from the user's message
CARGO REDIRECT — IMMEDIATE:
Then the chatbot displays:
"For Air Canada Cargo-related requests, please contact our travel experts using the phone number listed on the Air Canada Contact Us page."
And a clickable link labeled "Contact Us | Travel Info & Support" is displayed
And the chatbot shall NOT proceed to booking authentication
And the chatbot shall NOT ask for PNR or surname
And the chatbot shall NOT display legal disclaimer for BCInvol
And no identification questions shall be asked
And no OTP verification shall occur
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
Chatbot immediately identifies the AC Cargo-related context from the user's message.

Cargo contact message is displayed with correct verbatim.

Clickable "Contact Us | Travel Info & Support" link is displayed and functional — redirects to AC Cargo contact page.

• Passenger: Ethan Walker
• PNR: GR5M9Q

Given Ethan Walker has Air Canada booking PNR GR5M9Q for flight AC126 Vancouver → Calgary on 20-Jun-2026
And PNR contains GRPS SSR (group booking)
And the flight has been cancelled
And Ethan opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Ethan accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Ethan Walker"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Ethan! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR GR5M9Q"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR GR5M9Q

Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: e***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: e***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."

Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for PNR "GR5M9Q"
And the system validates booking eligibility
And Eligibility API confirms disruption exists

Then the chatbot shall display:
"I'm sorry, this flight is a group booking so I can't help you change your flight. To change your flight, you need to contact your booking source."

And no "Confirm proposed itinerary" option is shown
And no "Select a new flight" option is shown
And no "Cancel my booking" option is shown
And the flow shall terminate
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

GRPS SSR correctly detected.

Group booking message displayed: "I'm sorry, this flight is a group booking so I can't help you change your flight. To change your flight, you need to contact your booking source."

Self-service blocked. Flow terminates.
