📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that a disrupted booking created through the Employee Travel Site (ETS) is identified as ineligible for involuntary self-service rebooking per booking source eligibility rules.

ETS bookings use patterns **ES, **EP, **OT, **EC.

Preconditions:
• Passenger: David Reynolds
• PNR: ET5K9M
• Original Flight: AC302 YYZ → YOW — Cancelled (UN)
• Booking source: ETS (pattern **ES)
• Disruption > 30 minutes ago

Given David Reynolds has Air Canada booking PNR ET5K9M for flight AC302 Toronto → Ottawa on 20-Jun-2026
And the flight has been cancelled (segment status = UN)
And booking source = ETS (Employee Travel Site, pattern **ES)
And disruption occurred > 30 minutes ago
And David opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And David accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "David Reynolds"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, David! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR ET5K9M"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR ET5K9M
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: d***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: d***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)

"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."

Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for PNR "ET5K9M"
And DBaaS validates booking source — ETS pattern (**ES) detected
And bookingSourceEligibility.isEligible = false
ETS RESTRICTION:
Then chatbot shall display ETS booking source restriction message:
"We're working on it! We're looking at options to rebook you right now, you should receive a notification of your rebooking within the next 30 minutes."
And self-service BCInvol options shall NOT be displayed
And no "Confirm proposed itinerary" option is shown
And no "Select a new flight" option is shown
And no "Cancel my booking" option is shown
And flow shall terminate
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

ETS booking source correctly identified as ineligible for INVOL.

Self-service blocked.

Flow terminates with appropriate messaging.

Verify Live Agent Hand-off Live Agent Handoff triggered.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that bookings containing UMNR (Unaccompanied Minor) SSR are blocked from BCInvol self-service and routed to live agent.

UMNR passengers require specialized handling — rebooking must ensure minor supervision protocols are maintained.

Preconditions:
• Passenger: Lily Turner (age 10, CHD)
• PNR: UM4K7R
• Original Flight: AC188 YVR → YYC — Cancelled (UN)
• Auto-rebooked on AC192 (HK)
• SSR: UMNR (Unaccompanied Minor)
• Booking source: ACB

Given passenger Lily Turner (age 10, CHD) has Air Canada booking PNR UM4K7R for flight AC188 Vancouver → Calgary on 20-Jun-2026
And flight AC188 has been cancelled (segment status = UN)
And Lily is auto-rebooked on AC192 (segment status = HK)
And PNR contains UMNR SSR (Unaccompanied Minor)
And booking source is ACB
And Lily's guardian opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My child's flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And the guardian accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Lily Turner"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Lily! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR UM4K7R"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR UM4K7R
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: l***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: l***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
JOURNEY SELECTION:
Then the chatbot retrieves booking details and displays:
"I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC 188
• Route: Vancouver (YVR) → Calgary (YYC)
• Date: 20-Jun-2026
Is this the journey you'd like to check?"
And the user confirms "Yes"
And the system validates booking eligibility successfully
And BCINVOL eligibility returns ELIGIBLE
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."
Then the chatbot checks if the passenger have a new rebooking → Yes
Then the chatbot responds: "Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight."
And new itinerary comparison is displayed with Old Flight card (AC188 — Cancelled) And New Flight card (AC192 — Confirmed)
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"
And chatbot displays three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And the guardian selects "Select a new flight"
SELF-SERVE CHECKS — CHECK 1 (BAGGAGE):
Then the chatbot checks if the user is self-serve eligible based on baggage → Yes (no checked baggage)
SELF-SERVE CHECKS — CHECK 2 (SSR/PET):
Then System performs Self-Serve Checks Check 2 (SSR/Pet) is triggered
And system detects UMNR SSR on the booking
And UMNR SSR shall block self-service
UMNR RESTRICTION:
Then chatbot shall display:
"I can help you initiate a booking change. To better assist you, we'll need to rebook the flight manually. Let me transfer to a live agent who can help you."
And chatbot shall NOT proceed to Date Selection & Flight Search (alternate flight search)
And Live Agent Handoff shall be triggered
And agent routing options shall be displayed
And critical child safety handling preserved

Expected Outcome:
UMNR SSR correctly detected.

Self-service blocked.

Live agent handoff initiated.

Critical child safety handling preserved.

• Disruption > 30 minutes ago

And booking source = ACB

Description: Validate that bookings containing AVIH (Animal in Hold) SSR are blocked from BCInvol self-service.

Preconditions:
• Passenger: Maria Santos
• PNR: AH6K9P
• Original Flight: AC300 YYZ → YVR — Cancelled (UN)
• Auto-rebooked on AC310 (HK)
• SSR: AVIH (Animal in Hold)
• Booking source: ACB

Given Maria Santos has Air Canada booking PNR AH6K9P for flight AC300 Toronto → Vancouver on 20-Jun-2026
And flight AC300 has been cancelled (segment status = UN)
And Maria is auto-rebooked on AC310 (segment status = HK)

And booking source is ACB

Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"

Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Maria accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Maria Santos"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Maria! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR AH6K9P"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR AH6K9P
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: m***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: m***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
JOURNEY SELECTION:
Then the chatbot retrieves booking details and displays:
"I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC 300
• Route: Toronto (YYZ) → Vancouver (YVR)
• Date: 20-Jun-2026
Is this the journey you'd like to check?"
And the user confirms "Yes"
And the system validates booking eligibility successfully
And BCINVOL eligibility returns ELIGIBLE
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."
Then the chatbot checks if the passenger have a new rebooking → Yes
Then the chatbot responds: "Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight."
And new itinerary comparison is displayed with Old Flight card (AC300 — Cancelled) And New Flight card (AC310 — Confirmed)
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"
And chatbot displays three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And Maria selects "Select a new flight"
SELF-SERVE CHECKS — CHECK 1 (BAGGAGE):
Then the chatbot checks if the user is self-serve eligible based on baggage → Yes (no checked baggage blocking)
SELF-SERVE CHECKS — CHECK 2 (SSR/PET):
Then System performs Self-Serve Checks Check 2 (SSR/Pet) is triggered
And system detects AVIH SSR on the booking
And AVIH SSR shall block self-service
AVIH RESTRICTION:
Then chatbot shall display:
"I can help you initiate a booking change. To better assist you, we'll need to rebook the flight manually and re-append your add-on services. Let me transfer you to an agent who can help you. If you're already at the airport, please speak with an Air Canada representative."
And chatbot shall NOT proceed to Date Selection & Flight Search (alternate flight search)
And Live Agent Handoff shall be triggered
And agent routing options shall be displayed

Expected Outcome:
AVIH SSR correctly detected.

Self-service blocked.

Live agent handoff initiated.

🔢 Numbered steps
1. Launch chatbot and authenticate (PNR RF2T7M + Martin).
2. Verify EIP disruption and rebooking details.
3. Validate DBaaS logic (ACB, not IT/BT, delay >179).
4. Confirm refund eligibility response fields.
5. Verify eligibility gate passes.
6. Validate 3 options displayed (Confirm, Change, Refund).
7. Select refund.
8. Verify deep link, messaging, and flow termination.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Passenger (Lucas Martin) 
Booking RF2T7M. 
Original flight AC500 cancelled (UN). Auto-rebooked to AC510 (HK). 
Arrival delay = 240 minutes (>179 min threshold). 
Booking source ACB, not IT/BT, not ETS/ACV. 
Eligible for involuntary refund. 
System validates eligibility via Order Retrieve API and DBaaS. 
Options displayed: Confirm, Change, Cancel & Refund. 
Selecting refund redirects to AC.com flow.

🔢 Numbered steps
1. Launch chatbot on mobile.
2. Authenticate booking.
3. Reach alternate flight step.
4. Close browser.
5. Open desktop browser.
6. Restore session.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that an in-progress BCInvol session can be resumed when the customer switches device, browser, or communication channel.

Preconditions:
• Passenger: Emily Carter
• PNR: SR4N8P
• Session persistence enabled

PHASE 1 — INITIAL SESSION ON MOBILE:
Given Emily Carter has Air Canada booking PNR SR4N8P for a disrupted flight on 20-Jun-2026
And Emily opens the Air Canada chatbot on her mobile browser
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Emily accepts the disclaimer

Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Emily Carter"
Then the chatbot responds:

And the user provides "PNR SR4N8P"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR SR4N8P
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)

Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"

• Email: e***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567

Then the chatbot responds:

And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
JOURNEY SELECTION & DISRUPTION DISPLAY:
Then the chatbot retrieves booking details and displays the disrupted journey
And the system validates booking eligibility successfully
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."
Then the chatbot checks if the passenger have a new rebooking → Yes
Then the chatbot responds: "Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight."
And new itinerary comparison is displayed
And the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"

• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking

ALTERNATE FLIGHT SELECTION — IN PROGRESS:
Then the chatbot responds: "Let's look into some alternative flights together. Please scroll and select your preferred new itinerary."
And the chatbot displays the flight options (Top 5 to start)
— AT THIS POINT: Emily is on the flight selection step —
— AT THIS POINT: Emily closes her mobile browser —
PHASE 2 — SESSION RECOVERY ON DESKTOP:
When Emily closes mobile browser
And Emily opens Air Canada chatbot on desktop browser
And Session Recovery Service identifies active session token for Emily's account
Then the chatbot shall restore the conversation state
And the chatbot displays a session recovery message (e.g., "Welcome back! I've restored your previous session.")
And Emily shall resume exactly from the flight selection step
And the alternate flight options are still displayed
And Emily does NOT need to re-enter PNR, surname, or OTP
And Emily does NOT need to re-accept the legal disclaimer
And all previous progress (authentication, eligibility, journey selection) is preserved
And Emily selects a flight from the displayed options
And the chatbot responds: "Got it! Here's a summary of the requested change:"
And the chatbot displays the new itinerary comparison
And the user confirms the change
And the chatbot asks: "Would you like to proceed?"
And the user responds: "Yes"
And the chatbot responds: "Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly."
And the chatbot mentions: "Here's an overview of your new booking:"
And the chatbot displays the new itinerary summary
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
Session restored successfully. No user progress lost.

It looks like we had a small disruption on our end. Can you please try again?

🤖
chatbot shall display escalation message:
🤖
I'm sorry, looks like we still have technical problems. Unfortunately, your progress was not saved. Please try again later. We are sorry for the inconvenience.
⚙️ Confirm API failure handled with retry → escalation pattern.
🔢 Numbered steps
Complete full INVOL flow through flight selection — select AC520.
Confirm selection.
Trigger Confirm Alternate Offer API failure (first attempt).
Verify retry message displayed.
Verify system retries automatically.
Trigger second API failure.
Verify escalation message displayed.
Verify Live Agent Hand-off Live Agent Handoff triggered.
Verify agent receives full context including selected flight.
Verify NO partial booking changes committed.
Verify original booking unchanged.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that when the Confirm Alternate Offer API fails during the final rebooking confirmation step, the system retries once, and if the retry also fails, escalates the customer to a live agent.

This is distinct from TC026 (eligibility API timeout) — this failure occurs AFTER the customer has already searched, selected, and confirmed an alternate flight.

Preconditions:
• Passenger: Nathan Clarke
• PNR: CF7A3K
• Original Flight: AC500 YYZ → YVR — Cancelled (UN)
• Auto-rebooked on AC510 (HK)
• Customer has passed all eligibility and System performs Self-Serve Checks checks
• Customer has selected alternate flight AC520
• Confirm Alternate Offer API will fail on invocation

Given Nathan Clarke has Air Canada booking PNR CF7A3K for flight AC500 Toronto → Vancouver on 20-Jun-2026
And flight AC500 has been cancelled (segment status = UN)
And Nathan is auto-rebooked on AC510 (segment status = HK)
And Nathan opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Nathan accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Nathan Clarke"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Nathan! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR CF7A3K"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR CF7A3K
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: n***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: n***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
JOURNEY SELECTION:
Then the chatbot retrieves booking details and displays:
"I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC 500
• Route: Toronto (YYZ) → Vancouver (YVR)
• Date: 20-Jun-2026
Is this the journey you'd like to check?"
And the user confirms "Yes"
And the system validates booking eligibility successfully
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."
Then the chatbot checks if the passenger have a new rebooking → Yes
Then the chatbot responds: "Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight."
And new itinerary comparison is displayed with Old Flight card (AC500 — Cancelled) And New Flight card (AC510 — Confirmed)
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"
And chatbot displays three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And Nathan selects "Select a new flight"
Then the chatbot checks if the user is self-serve eligible based on baggage → Yes
Then the chatbot checks if the user have SSRs → No
Then the chatbot checks if the user have special meals → No
ALTERNATE FLIGHT SELECTION (INVOL):
Then the chatbot responds: "Let's look into some alternative flights together. Please scroll and select your preferred new itinerary."
And the chatbot displays the flight options (Top 5 to start)
And Nathan selects alternate flight AC520
And the chatbot responds: "Got it! Here's a summary of the requested change:"
And the chatbot displays the new itinerary comparison
And Nathan confirms the change
And the chatbot asks: "Would you like to proceed?"
And Nathan responds: "Yes"
CONFIRM API FAILURE — ATTEMPT 1:
When the system calls Confirm Alternate Offer API
And the API fails (timeout / HTTP 500 / service error)
Then chatbot shall display first retry message:
"It looks like we had a small disruption on our end. Can you please try again?"
CONFIRM API FAILURE — ATTEMPT 2 (AUTOMATIC RETRY):
When the system retries Confirm Alternate Offer API automatically
And the retry also fails (second timeout / failure)
Then chatbot shall display escalation message:
"I'm sorry, looks like we still have technical problems. Unfortunately, your progress was not saved. Please try again later. We are sorry for the inconvenience."
And Live Agent Handoff shall be triggered
And agent routing options shall be displayed
And NO partial booking changes shall be committed
And original booking shall remain unchanged (AC510 = HK)
And no ticket reissue shall occur

Expected Outcome:
Confirm API failure handled with retry → escalation pattern.

Customer escalated to live agent after second failure.

No partial booking changes committed.

Original booking preserved.

📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate behavior when Service Error API detects outage BEFORE disclaimer screen.

Preconditions:
• Service Error API configured to return outage/downtime status

Given chatbot services are experiencing downtime
And Service Error API is configured to return outage status
PRE-ENTRY DOWNTIME CHECK:
When customer launches the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot shall call Service Error API before disclaimer
And Service Error API returns outage status
DOWNTIME MESSAGE:
Then the chatbot shall display downtime message informing the customer that services are temporarily unavailable
And legal disclaimer shall NOT be displayed
And no authentication shall occur
And no identification questions shall be asked
And no PNR entry field shall be shown
And no OTP verification shall occur
And no BCInvol self-service flow shall be initiated
And flow shall terminate immediately

Expected Outcome:
Service downtime detected before disclaimer.

Customer informed immediately.

No wasted effort or partial session creation.

🔢 Numbered steps
Launch the Air Canada chatbot.
Chatbot displays the legal disclaimer — click "I agree."
Enter PNR "MX3B7K" in the booking reference field.
Enter surname "Harris."
Verify Authentication API retrieves booking successfully with 3 bounds.
Complete OTP verification.
Verify Order Retrieve API is called and returns 3 independent bounds.
Verify the system classifies each bound:
Bound 1 (AC200 YYZ → YVR) = VOL eligible (no disruption)
Bound 2 (AC300 YVR → YYC) = VOL eligible (no disruption)
Bound 3 (AC400 YYC → YYZ) = INVOL eligible (cancelled, auto-rebooked on AC410)
Verify chatbot displays all 3 bounds with correct statuses — Bound 3 clearly marked as cancelled/disrupted.
Scenario A — Select Bound 3 (INVOL):
Click on Bound 3 (AC400 — cancelled).
Verify chatbot displays booking overview with original AC400 (cancelled) and rebooked AC410 (confirmed).
Verify ancillary services alert is displayed.
Verify three involuntary options are displayed: "Confirm proposed itinerary" | "Select a new flight" | "Cancel my booking."
Verify voluntary options ("Change my flight" / "Upgrade my flight") are NOT displayed.
Scenario B — Go back and select Bound 1 (VOL):
Return to bound selection screen.
Click on Bound 1 (AC200 — active/unflown).
Verify chatbot displays: "What kind of change are you looking for?"
Verify two voluntary options are displayed: "Change my flight" | "Upgrade my flight."
Verify involuntary options ("Confirm proposed itinerary" / "Select a new flight" / "Cancel my booking") are NOT displayed.
Verify bound classification is independent — selecting or modifying one bound does not re-classify or affect the eligibility of other bounds.
If customer selects "Select a new flight" for Bound 3, verify that AC410 (already-rebooked flight) does NOT appear in the alternate flight search results.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that when a single PNR contains 3 separate bounds (not connections — independent trips days apart), the system correctly evaluates each bound individually.

Bounds 1 and 2 are unflown and undisrupted (VOL eligible), while Bound 3 has been cancelled (INVOL eligible).

Preconditions:
• Passenger: Daniel Harris (ADT, single passenger)
• PNR: MX3B7K
• Bound 1: AC200 YYZ → YVR — VOL
• Bound 2: AC300 YVR → YYC — VOL
• Bound 3: AC400 YYC → YYZ — CANCELLED (INVOL)
• Booking source: ACB
• No blocking SSRs

Given passenger Daniel Harris has booking PNR "MX3B7K"
And the PNR contains 3 independent bounds:
Bound 1: AC200 YYZ → YVR on 20-Jun-2026 — UNFLOWN — No disruption (VOL)
Bound 2: AC300 YVR → YYC on 23-Jun-2026 — UNFLOWN — No disruption (VOL)
Bound 3: AC400 YYC → YYZ on 26-Jun-2026 — CANCELLED (UN) — Auto-rebooked AC410 (HK) (INVOL)
And booking source is ACB, ticket stock 014
And no blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, no special meals
And Daniel opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Daniel accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Daniel Harris"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Daniel! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR MX3B7K"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR MX3B7K
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: d***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: d***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
SYSTEM CLASSIFICATION:
Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for PNR "MX3B7K"
And the system evaluates each bound independently:
Bound 1: AC200 — isDisrupted = false — No cancellation — VOL eligible
Bound 2: AC300 — isDisrupted = false — No cancellation — VOL eligible
Bound 3: AC400 — isDisrupted = true — Status = UN — INVOL eligible
And the system identifies AC410 as auto-rebooked flight for Bound 3
BOUND DISPLAY:
Then the chatbot shall display all 3 bounds with their statuses:
Bound 1: AC200 YYZ → YVR — 20-Jun-2026 — Active
Bound 2: AC300 YVR → YYC — 23-Jun-2026 — Active
Bound 3: AC400 YYC → YYZ — 26-Jun-2026 — Cancelled
And the chatbot shall clearly indicate Bound 3 as disrupted
SCENARIO A — Customer selects disrupted Bound 3 (INVOL):
When Daniel selects Bound 3 (AC400 YYC → YYZ — CANCELLED)
Then the system shall identify this bound as involuntarily disrupted
And the chatbot shall display the booking overview showing:
Original flight AC400 (cancelled)
Rebooked flight AC410 (confirmed)
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
And the chatbot shall display three INVOL options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And the chatbot shall NOT display voluntary change options like "Change my flight" or "Upgrade my flight"
SCENARIO B — Customer goes back and selects undisrupted Bound 1 (VOL):
When Daniel goes back to bound selection
And Daniel selects Bound 1 (AC200 YYZ → YVR — UNFLOWN)
Then the system shall identify this bound as undisrupted and voluntary eligible
And the chatbot shall display:
"What kind of change are you looking for?"
And the chatbot shall display two VOL options:
• Change my flight
• Upgrade my flight
And the chatbot shall NOT display involuntary options like "Confirm proposed itinerary" or "Select a new flight" or "Cancel my booking"
CRITICAL VALIDATION:
Then bound classification shall remain independent:
Modifying Bound 1 (VOL) shall NOT affect Bound 3 (INVOL) classification
Modifying Bound 3 (INVOL) shall NOT affect Bound 1 or 2 (VOL) eligibility
And the already-rebooked flight AC410 for Bound 3 shall NOT appear in alternate flight search results if customer searches
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
System correctly identifies 3 bounds with mixed eligibility in one PNR.
Bound-level classification is independent: 2 VOL + 1 INVOL.
Selecting Bound 3 (INVOL) triggers involuntary disruption options — confirm rebooked itinerary, search alternate, or cancel/refund.
Selecting Bound 1 (VOL) triggers voluntary change options — change flight or upgrade.
No cross-contamination between bound eligibility.
Already-rebooked flight AC410 excluded from alternate search results.

🔢 Numbered steps
Launch the Air Canada chatbot.
Accept the legal disclaimer by clicking "I agree."
Enter PNR "MX5D9R" and surname "Clarke."
Verify booking retrieved with 3 bounds.
Complete OTP verification.
Verify system classifies Bound 1 = VOL, Bound 2 = INVOL, Bound 3 = INVOL.
Verify chatbot displays all 3 bounds — Bound 2 and 3 marked as cancelled/disrupted.
Select Bound 2 (INVOL — AC250 cancelled):
Verify booking overview shows AC250 (cancelled) and AC260 (rebooked).
Verify ancillary alert displayed.
Verify three options displayed: "Confirm proposed itinerary" | "Select a new flight" | "Cancel my booking."
Select "Select a new flight."
Verify system checks for checked baggage on Bound 2 — none found, passes.
Verify system checks for blocking SSRs on Bound 2 — none found, passes.
Verify system checks for special meals on Bound 2 — none found, passes.
Verify date picker displayed with ±3 day range.
Select original departure date.
Verify Alternative Flight Search API returns flights — AC260 (already-rebooked) is NOT in results.
Select alternate flight AC265.
Verify itinerary comparison displayed: AC250 (cancelled) vs AC265 (selected).
Confirm selection.
Verify Confirm Alternate Offer API succeeds — Bound 2 updated to AC265.
Verify ticket coupon reissued at no cost for Bound 2.
Return to bound selection.
Select Bound 3 (INVOL — AC350 cancelled):
Verify booking overview shows AC350 (cancelled) and AC360 (rebooked).
Verify three options displayed.
Verify Bound 2 change (AC265) is preserved — not reverted.
Select "Confirm proposed itinerary."
Verify Acknowledge Itinerary API succeeds — Bound 3 confirmed with AC360.
Verify Bound 1 (AC100 — VOL) remains completely unchanged.
Verify confirmation email sent covering all changes.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that when 2 out of 3 bounds are involuntarily disrupted, the system correctly classifies both as INVOL and allows the customer to address each disrupted bound independently without confusing flow logic. Resolving one disrupted bound must not affect the other.

 Preconditions: • Passenger: Emma Clarke (ADT) • PNR: MX5D9R • Bound 1: AC100 YUL → YOW on 18-Jun-2026 — UNFLOWN, no disruption (VOL) • Bound 2: AC250 YOW → YYZ on 21-Jun-2026 — CANCELLED (UN), auto-rebooked AC260 (HK) (INVOL) • Bound 3: AC350 YYZ → YHZ on 24-Jun-2026 — CANCELLED (UN), auto-rebooked AC360 (HK) (INVOL) • Booking source: ACB, ticket stock: 014 • No blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, no special meals • Both disruptions > 30 minutes ago

Given passenger Emma Clarke has booking PNR "MX5D9R"
And the PNR contains 3 independent bounds:
Bound 1: AC100 YUL → YOW on 18-Jun-2026 — UNFLOWN — No disruption (VOL)
Bound 2: AC250 YOW → YYZ on 21-Jun-2026 — CANCELLED (UN) — Rebooked AC260 (HK) (INVOL)
Bound 3: AC350 YYZ → YHZ on 24-Jun-2026 — CANCELLED (UN) — Rebooked AC360 (HK) (INVOL)
And both disruptions occurred > 30 minutes ago
And booking source is ACB, ticket stock 014
And no blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, no special meals
And Emma opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Emma accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Emma Clarke"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Emma! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR MX5D9R"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR MX5D9R
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: e***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: e***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
SYSTEM CLASSIFICATION:
Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for PNR "MX5D9R"
And the system evaluates each bound independently:
Bound 1: AC100 — isDisrupted = false — VOL eligible
Bound 2: AC250 — isDisrupted = true — INVOL eligible
Bound 3: AC350 — isDisrupted = true — INVOL eligible
BOUND DISPLAY:
Then chatbot shall display all 3 bounds with statuses:
Bound 1: AC100 YUL → YOW — Active
Bound 2: AC250 YOW → YYZ — Cancelled
Bound 3: AC350 YYZ → YHZ — Cancelled
And chatbot shall clearly indicate Bound 2 and Bound 3 as disrupted
PROCESS BOUND 2 (INVOL):
When Emma selects Bound 2 (AC250 — cancelled)
Then chatbot shall display booking overview:
Original: AC250 YOW → YYZ (cancelled)
Rebooked: AC260 YOW → YYZ (confirmed)
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
And chatbot shall display three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
When Emma selects "Select a new flight"
Then the chatbot checks if the user is self-serve eligible based on baggage → Yes (no checked baggage on Bound 2)
Then the chatbot checks if the user have SSRs → No (no blocking SSRs on Bound 2)
Then the chatbot checks if the user have special meals → No (no special meals on Bound 2)
Then the chatbot responds: "Let's look into some alternative flights together. Please scroll and select your preferred new itinerary."
And chatbot displays date picker within ±3 days
And Emma selects original departure date
And Alternative Flight Search API returns available flights
And AC260 (already-rebooked) is excluded from results
And Emma selects alternate flight AC265
And the chatbot responds: "Got it! Here's a summary of the requested change:"
And the chatbot displays the new itinerary comparison: AC250 (cancelled) vs AC265 (selected)
And Emma confirms the change
And the chatbot asks: "Would you like to proceed?"
And Emma responds: "Yes"
And Confirm Alternate Offer API succeeds
Then Bound 2 shall be updated: AC250 → XX, AC265 → HK
And ticket coupon for Bound 2 shall be reissued at no cost
And confirmation shall be displayed
PROCESS BOUND 3 (INVOL):
When Emma returns to bound selection
And Emma selects Bound 3 (AC350 — cancelled)
Then chatbot shall display booking overview:
Original: AC350 YYZ → YHZ (cancelled)
Rebooked: AC360 YYZ → YHZ (confirmed)
And chatbot shall display three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And Bound 2 resolution (AC265) shall be preserved and unchanged
When Emma selects "Confirm proposed itinerary"
And Acknowledge Itinerary API succeeds
Then Bound 3 shall be confirmed with AC360
And confirmation shall be displayed
FINAL VERIFICATION:
Then Bound 1 (AC100 — VOL) shall remain unchanged and still VOL eligible
And Bound 2 shall show AC265 (confirmed via alternate search)
And Bound 3 shall show AC360 (confirmed via acknowledgment)
And all 3 bounds shall be independently resolved
And confirmation email shall be sent covering all changes
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
Multiple disruptions in one PNR handled correctly.
Each INVOL bound processed independently — Bound 2 via alternate search, Bound 3 via acknowledgment.
Resolving Bound 2 does not affect Bound 3 or vice versa.
VOL Bound 1 remains eligible and unchanged throughout.
All tickets reissued correctly. Confirmation email sent.

Launch chatbot and accept legal disclaimer.
Enter PNR "MX7F2P" and surname "Taylor."
Complete OTP verification.
Verify all 3 bounds retrieved — all classified as INVOL (cancelled).

Process Bound 1:
Select Bound 1 (AC150 — cancelled).

Select "Confirm proposed itinerary."
Verify Acknowledge API succeeds — Bound 1 confirmed with AC155.
Return to bound selection.
Process Bound 2:
Select Bound 2 (AC280 — cancelled).
Verify Bound 1 resolution preserved.
Select "Select a new flight."
Verify checked baggage check passes (no bags).
Verify SSR/Pet check passes (no blocking SSRs).
Verify special meal check passes (no meals).
Select date within ±3 days.
Verify AC285 (already-rebooked) excluded from search results.
Select alternate flight AC290.
Confirm selection.
Verify Confirm Alternate Offer API succeeds — Bound 2 updated to AC290.
Verify ticket reissued at no cost.
Return to bound selection.
Process Bound 3:
Select Bound 3 (AC380 — cancelled).
Verify Bound 1 (AC155) and Bound 2 (AC290) resolutions preserved.
Select "Cancel my booking."
Verify refund alert displayed.
Verify "Cancel my flight" deep-link appears after 60-second delay.
Click deep-link — verify redirect to AC.com refund flow.
Verify all 3 bounds resolved independently — no cross-bound interference.
Verify no system errors throughout the process.
Verify confirmation email sent.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Worst-case stress scenario: all 3 independent bounds are cancelled. Validates system doesn't crash, confuse bounds, or misroute flows when every bound requires involuntary processing.

 Preconditions: • Passenger: Mark Taylor (ADT) • PNR: MX7F2P • Bound 1: AC150 YYZ → YUL on 19-Jun-2026 — CANCELLED (UN), rebooked AC155 (HK) • Bound 2: AC280 YUL → YOW on 22-Jun-2026 — CANCELLED (UN), rebooked AC285 (HK) • Bound 3: AC380 YOW → YVR on 25-Jun-2026 — CANCELLED (UN), rebooked AC385 (HK) • Booking source: ACB, ticket stock: 014 • All disruptions > 30 minutes ago • No blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, no special meals

Given passenger Mark Taylor has booking PNR "MX7F2P"
And the PNR contains 3 independent bounds — ALL cancelled:
Bound 1: AC150 YYZ → YUL on 19-Jun-2026 — CANCELLED (UN) — Rebooked AC155 (HK)
Bound 2: AC280 YUL → YOW on 22-Jun-2026 — CANCELLED (UN) — Rebooked AC285 (HK)
Bound 3: AC380 YOW → YVR on 25-Jun-2026 — CANCELLED (UN) — Rebooked AC385 (HK)
And all disruptions occurred > 30 minutes ago

And no blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, no special meals
And Mark opens the Air Canada chatbot

Then user selects English

Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."

Bound 2: AC280 — isDisrupted = true — INVOL eligible

Then chatbot shall display all 3 bounds — all marked as cancelled/disrupted

PROCESS BOUND 1 — ACKNOWLEDGMENT:
When Mark selects Bound 1 (AC150 — cancelled)
Then chatbot shall display booking overview:
Original: AC150 YYZ → YUL (cancelled)
Rebooked: AC155 YYZ → YUL (confirmed)
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
And chatbot shall display three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking

And Acknowledge Itinerary API succeeds
Then Bound 1 shall be confirmed with AC155
And confirmation shall be displayed
PROCESS BOUND 2 — ALTERNATE SEARCH:
When Mark returns to bound selection
And Mark selects Bound 2 (AC280 — cancelled)
Then chatbot shall display booking overview:
Original: AC280 YUL → YOW (cancelled)
Rebooked: AC285 YUL → YOW (confirmed)

And chatbot shall display three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
When Mark selects "Select a new flight"
Then the chatbot checks if the user is self-serve eligible based on baggage → Yes (no checked baggage)

• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
When Mark selects "Cancel my booking"
Then the chatbot responds: "Please follow this link to initiate a refund"
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note we can only issue you a refund for any unflown segments of your trip."
And after a 60-second delay a deep link with a "Cancel my flight" button is displayed
And Mark clicks on the "Cancel my flight" button
Then Mark shall be redirected to AC.com cancellation/refund flow for Bound 3
FINAL VERIFICATION:
Then Bound 1 = AC155 (confirmed via acknowledgment)
And Bound 2 = AC290 (confirmed via alternate search)
And Bound 3 = refund initiated via deep-link
And all resolutions shall be independent and preserved
And no system errors shall have occurred
And confirmation email shall be sent
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
Triple disruption handled without system error.
Each bound processed independently: Bound 1 via acknowledgment, Bound 2 via alternate search, Bound 3 via refund.
All resolutions preserved across sequential processing.
No cross-bound contamination or flow confusion.

Launch chatbot and accept legal disclaimer.
Enter PNR "SD4V8K" and surname "Kim."

Verify check-in validation passes (not checked in).

Select same date in date picker.
Verify alternate flights returned.
Select AC405 (10:00 EDT).

Confirm change.
Click payment link and complete payment of CAD 75.
Return to chatbot.
Verify Bound 1 updated to AC405.
Verify Bound 2 (AC420) remains unchanged.
Change Return (Bound 2):
Return to bound selection.
Select Bound 2 (AC420 — 19:00).
Verify voluntary options displayed again.
Select "Change my flight."
Select AC425 (21:00 EDT).

Confirm and complete payment.
Verify Bound 2 updated to AC425.
Verify Bound 1 change (AC405) preserved.
Verify both coupons reissued.
Verify confirmation email reflects both changes.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that when a customer has a same-day roundtrip (outbound morning, return evening) and both bounds are undisrupted, the system allows voluntary changes on both bounds independently. 

Preconditions: • Passenger: Rachel Kim (ADT) • PNR: SD4V8K • Bound 1 (Outbound): AC401 YYZ → YUL — 25-Jun-2026 08:00 EDT — UNFLOWN (VOL) • Bound 2 (Return): AC420 YUL → YYZ — 25-Jun-2026 19:00 EDT — UNFLOWN (VOL) • Booking source: ACB, ticket stock: 014, Fare: Flex Economy • No blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, not checked in • Within 72-hour departure window

Given passenger Rachel Kim has booking PNR "SD4V8K"
And the PNR contains same-day roundtrip:
Bound 1: AC401 YYZ → YUL on 25-Jun-2026 at 08:00 EDT — UNFLOWN (VOL)
Bound 2: AC420 YUL → YYZ on 25-Jun-2026 at 19:00 EDT — UNFLOWN (VOL)
And no disruptions exist on either bound
And booking source is ACB, fare is Flex Economy
And no blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, not checked in
And Rachel opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user says "I'd like to change my flight"
When the chatbot identifies booking change intent
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Rachel accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Rachel Kim"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Rachel! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR SD4V8K"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR SD4V8K
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: r***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: r***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
SYSTEM CLASSIFICATION:
Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for PNR "SD4V8K"
And the system evaluates both bounds:
Bound 1: AC401 — isDisrupted = false — VOL eligible
Bound 2: AC420 — isDisrupted = false — VOL eligible
And the system validates eligibility Rules 1–8 for both bounds — all pass
Then chatbot shall display both bounds for selection
CHANGE OUTBOUND (BOUND 1):
When Rachel selects Bound 1 (AC401 YYZ → YUL — 08:00)
Then chatbot shall display: "What kind of change are you looking for?"
And chatbot shall display:
• Change my flight
• Upgrade my flight
When Rachel selects "Change my flight"
Then the system validates check-in status — not checked in, passes
And the system detects single passenger — bypasses multi-pax check
And chatbot displays: "Let's look into some alternative flights together! What didn't work for you about your flight?"
And chatbot displays options:
• Date/Time
• Arrival/Departure Airport
And Rachel selects "Date/Time"
Then chatbot displays date picker within ±7 day window
And Rachel selects same date 25-Jun-2026
And Flight Availability API returns alternate flights
And Rachel selects flight AC405 departing 10:00 EDT
Then chatbot responds: "Please choose a fare class from the options below:"
And Rachel selects the fare class
Then the chatbot responds: "Got it! Here is a summary of requested change:"
And chatbot displays itinerary comparison:
Old: AC401 at 08:00
New: AC405 at 10:00
Fare difference: CAD $75
And Rachel confirms the change
And chatbot generates payment link inside active session
And Rachel completes payment of CAD $75 on external payment page
And Rachel returns to chatbot
Then Bound 1 shall be updated: AC401 → AC405
And ticket coupon for Bound 1 shall be reissued
And Bound 2 (AC420 at 19:00) shall remain completely unchanged
And the chatbot responds: "Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly."
CHANGE RETURN (BOUND 2):
When Rachel returns to bound selection
And Rachel selects Bound 2 (AC420 YUL → YYZ — 19:00)

• Change my flight
• Upgrade my flight
When Rachel selects "Change my flight"

Then the chatbot responds: "Got it! Here is a summary of requested change:"

Old: AC420 at 19:00
New: AC425 at 21:00

And chatbot generates payment link

Then Bound 2 shall be updated: AC420 → AC425

And Bound 1 change (AC405) shall be preserved — not reverted
And the chatbot responds: "Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly."
FINAL VERIFICATION:

Bound 1: AC405 YYZ → YUL — 10:00 — Confirmed
Bound 2: AC425 YUL → YYZ — 21:00 — Confirmed
And both ticket coupons shall be reissued
And confirmation email shall reflect both changes
And total payment collected = CAD $125 (CAD $75 + CAD $50)
END FLOW (GLOB-11):

• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
Both same-day bounds changed voluntarily in sequence.
Each bound's fare difference and payment calculated independently (CAD 75 + CAD 50).
Changes preserved across sequential processing — no cross-bound interference.
Both tickets reissued. Confirmation email sent.

📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that when a customer has a same-day roundtrip and BOTH flights are cancelled, the system correctly classifies both as INVOL and allows the customer to handle each disruption independently. 
Preconditions: • Passenger: Tom Bradley (ADT) • PNR: SD6I3R • Bound 1 (Outbound): AC401 YYZ → YUL — 25-Jun-2026 08:00 — CANCELLED (UN), rebooked AC405 (HK) • Bound 2 (Return): AC420 YUL → YYZ — 25-Jun-2026 19:00 — CANCELLED (UN), rebooked AC425 (HK) • Both disruptions > 30 minutes ago • No blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, no special meals

Given passenger Tom Bradley has booking PNR "SD6I3R"
And the PNR contains same-day roundtrip — BOTH cancelled:
Bound 1: AC401 YYZ → YUL at 08:00 — CANCELLED (UN) — Rebooked AC405 (HK)
Bound 2: AC420 YUL → YYZ at 19:00 — CANCELLED (UN) — Rebooked AC425 (HK)
And both disruptions occurred > 30 minutes ago
And no blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, no special meals
And Tom opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."

Rebooked: AC405 YYZ → YUL (confirmed)

• Select a new flight

Bound 2: AC425 — Confirmed via acknowledgment

Same-day dual disruption handled correctly.
Both bounds processed independently — Bound 1 via alternate search, Bound 2 via acknowledgment.
Both rebooked at no cost (involuntary waiver).
No cross-bound interference. Confirmation email sent.

🔢 Numbered steps
Launch chatbot and accept legal disclaimer.
Enter PNR "SS8A4K" and surname "Martin."
Complete OTP verification.
Verify Bound 1 = INVOL (cancelled, no SSRs) and Bound 2 = VOL (ABIH SSR attached).
Verify chatbot displays both bounds.
Select Bound 1 (INVOL — AC500 cancelled):
Verify booking overview: AC500 (cancelled) and AC510 (rebooked).
Verify three involuntary options displayed.
Select "Select a new flight."
Verify self-serve checks run on Bound 1 ONLY:
Checked baggage check: Verify system checks Bound 1 only — no bags found → passes.
SSR/Pet check: Verify system checks SSRs on Bound 1 segments only — NO SSRs found → passes.
CRITICAL: Verify ABIH SSR on Bound 2 is NOT evaluated against Bound 1. The system must use bound-level SSR evaluation, not PNR-level.
Special meal check: Verify system checks Bound 1 only — no meals → passes.
Verify chatbot proceeds to date selection (all 3 checks passed).
Select departure date within ±3 days.
Verify alternate flights returned — AC510 (already-rebooked) excluded.
Select alternate flight AC515.
Verify itinerary comparison displayed.
Confirm selection.
Verify Confirm Alternate Offer API succeeds.
Verify Bound 1 updated to AC515 at no cost.
Verify Bound 2 ABIH SSR remains attached and unchanged.
Verify Bound 2 (AC520) remains active/unflown and unaffected.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that when a blocking SSR (ABIH) exists ONLY on the return bound, the system correctly evaluates SSRs at the bound level, NOT the PNR level. The outbound (which is INVOL disrupted and has NO ABIH) should proceed through self-service without being blocked by the return's SSR.

 Preconditions: • Passenger: Sophie Martin (ADT) • PNR: SS8A4K • Bound 1 (Outbound): AC500 YYZ → YVR — CANCELLED (UN), rebooked AC510 (HK) — NO SSRs — INVOL • Bound 2 (Return): AC520 YVR → YYZ — UNFLOWN — ABIH SSR attached — VOL • Booking source: ACB, ticket stock: 014 • No checked baggage, no special meals • Disruption on Bound 1 > 30 minutes ago

Given passenger Sophie Martin has booking PNR "SS8A4K"
And Bound 1 (outbound): AC500 YYZ → YVR — CANCELLED (UN), rebooked AC510 (HK) — NO SSRs
And Bound 2 (return): AC520 YVR → YYZ — UNFLOWN — SSR ABIH attached
And disruption on Bound 1 > 30 minutes ago
And no checked baggage, no special meals
And Sophie opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Sophie accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Sophie Martin"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Sophie! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR SS8A4K"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR SS8A4K
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: s***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: s***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
SYSTEM CLASSIFICATION:
Then the system evaluates both bounds:
Bound 1: AC500 — INVOL — No SSRs — Eligible for self-service
Bound 2: AC520 — VOL — SSR ABIH — SSR may block at VOL flow
Then chatbot shall display both bounds with statuses
CUSTOMER SELECTS BOUND 1 (INVOL — NO SSR):
When Sophie selects Bound 1 (AC500 — cancelled, no SSRs)
Then chatbot shall display booking overview:
Original: AC500 YYZ → YVR (cancelled)
Rebooked: AC510 YYZ → YVR (confirmed)
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
And chatbot shall display three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
When Sophie selects "Select a new flight"
SELF-SERVE CHECKS — BOUND 1 ONLY:
Then the chatbot checks if the user is self-serve eligible based on baggage → Check 1 runs on Bound 1 only — no checked baggage found on Bound 1 → PASS
Then the chatbot checks if the user have SSRs → Check 2 (SSR/Pet) runs on Bound 1 segments ONLY — NO SSRs found on Bound 1 (ABIH is on Bound 2 only) → PASS
CRITICAL: ABIH on Bound 2 is NOT evaluated against Bound 1 — the system must NOT scan PNR-level SSRs — only bound-level
Then the chatbot checks if the user have special meals → Check 3 runs on Bound 1 only — no special meals found → PASS
ALTERNATE FLIGHT SELECTION:
Then the chatbot responds: "Let's look into some alternative flights together. Please scroll and select your preferred new itinerary."
And chatbot displays date picker within ±3 days
And Sophie selects original departure date
And Alternative Flight Search API returns available flights
And AC510 (already-rebooked) is excluded from results
And Sophie selects alternate flight AC515
And the chatbot responds: "Got it! Here's a summary of the requested change:"
And the chatbot displays itinerary comparison: AC500 (cancelled) vs AC515 (selected)
And Sophie confirms the selection
And the chatbot asks: "Would you like to proceed?"
And Sophie responds: "Yes"
And Confirm Alternate Offer API succeeds
Then Bound 1 shall be updated: AC500 → XX, AC515 → HK
And ticket coupon for Bound 1 shall be reissued at no cost
And the chatbot responds: "Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly."
And the chatbot mentions: "Here's an overview of your new booking:"
And the chatbot displays the new itinerary summary
VERIFICATION:
And Bound 2 SSR ABIH shall remain attached and unchanged
And Bound 2 eligibility shall not be affected by Bound 1 changes
And Bound 2 shall still show AC520 YVR → YYZ as active/unflown
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
ABIH SSR on Bound 2 does NOT block self-service for Bound 1.
SSR evaluation is bound-level, not PNR-level.
All 3 self-serve checks (baggage, SSR/Pet, special meal) evaluate Bound 1 only and pass.
Bound 1 (INVOL, no SSRs) proceeds through full self-service — date selection, flight search, confirmation, ticket reissue.
Bound 2 ABIH SSR preserved, unaffected by Bound 1 changes.

The test must document what actually happens when a passenger takes no action and contacts chatbot hours after the missed departure.

🔢 Numbered steps
Setup: Cancel AC600, auto-rebook on AC610, wait until AC610 departs. Take no action as the passenger.
Launch chatbot 5+ hours after AC610 departure.
Accept legal disclaimer.
Enter PNR "NA7K3M" and surname "Cooper."
Complete OTP verification.
Verify booking retrieved successfully.
Verify system identifies:
AC600 = cancelled (UN)
AC610 = departed without James (no-show or status unclear)
Record exact system behavior — which of the following occurs:
a) Involuntary self-service options still available (select new flight / cancel)?
b) Expired window message displayed?
c) No-show message displayed?
d) Live agent handoff triggered?
e) Error or unexpected behavior?
Verify no automatic booking changes committed.
Verify system does NOT assume James flew AC610.
Document actual behavior for team review with Fady/Parul.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate system behavior when a passenger's flight is involuntarily cancelled, the passenger takes NO action (does not rebook, does not acknowledge, does not cancel), the original flight departure time passes, the auto-rebooked flight also departs without the passenger, and the passenger contacts the chatbot several hours after. 

Preconditions: • Passenger: James Cooper (ADT) • PNR: NA7K3M • Original Flight: AC600 YYZ → YVR — CANCELLED (UN) at 08:00 EDT on 25-Jun-2026 • Auto-rebooked on AC610 — HK — departed at 10:00 EDT • James took NO action — did not fly AC610, did not contact AC • Current time: 25-Jun-2026 15:00 EDT (5 hours after AC610 departure) • No checked baggage, no blocking SSRs

Given passenger James Cooper has booking PNR "NA7K3M"
And original flight AC600 YYZ → YVR was cancelled at 08:00 EDT on 25-Jun-2026
And Air Canada auto-rebooked James on AC610 departing 10:00 EDT
And James took NO action — did not board AC610, did not contact Air Canada
And AC610 has already departed (5 hours ago)
And current time is 25-Jun-2026 15:00 EDT
And James now contacts the chatbot for the first time
And James opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And James accepts the disclaimer

"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "James Cooper"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, James! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR NA7K3M"

And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"

• Phone: +1***567

SYSTEM EVALUATION:
Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for PNR "NA7K3M"
And the system evaluates segment statuses:
AC600 (original) — Status: UN (cancelled)
AC610 (rebooked) — Status: departed without James (no-show or UN/FLOWN)
SYSTEM BEHAVIOR — DOCUMENT ACTUAL OUTCOME:
Then one of the following outcomes shall occur:
Possible Outcome A — Self-service still available:
If INVOL eligibility is still valid (disruption flag active and system allows post-departure rebooking)
Then chatbot may still offer involuntary options:
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
Possible Outcome B — Window expired:
If the self-service window has closed
Then chatbot shall display message indicating the rebooking window has expired
And chatbot shall route to live agent handoff
Possible Outcome C — No-show handling:
If system detects no-show on AC610
Then chatbot shall display appropriate no-show messaging
And may offer refund options or agent support
In ALL outcomes:
And no booking change shall be auto-committed
And system shall not assume James flew on AC610
NOTE: This is an exploratory/discovery test case — the exact expected behavior needs confirmation from the team. The test must document what actually happens when a passenger takes no action and contacts chatbot hours after the missed departure.
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
This is an exploratory/discovery test case — the exact expected behavior needs confirmation from Fady/Parul.
The test must document what actually happens when a passenger takes no action and contacts chatbot hours after the missed departure.
At minimum: system should NOT assume the passenger flew, should NOT auto-commit changes, and should provide a clear path forward (either self-service if still valid, or agent handoff).
