🔢 Numbered steps
Launch the Air Canada chatbot.
Accept the legal disclaimer by clicking "I agree."
Enter PNR "ST2H8K" and surname "Chen."
Verify Authentication API retrieves booking successfully.
Complete OTP verification.
Verify eligibility Rules 1–8 pass — booking marked VOL Eligible.
Chatbot displays "What kind of change are you looking for?" — select "Change my flight."
Verify check-in validation passes (not checked in).
Verify single-passenger detected — multi-pax check bypassed.
Chatbot displays change reason options — select "Date/Time."
Select new travel date 24-Jun-2026 in the date picker.
Verify Flight Availability API returns alternate flights.
Select flight AC210 with fare difference CAD 150.
Verify itinerary comparison displayed: AC200 (old) vs AC210 (new), fare CAD 150.
DO NOT click "Confirm." Leave the chatbot session open without any further action.
Wait until the session reaches the 2-hour mark. (Note: This may require test environment configuration to accelerate timeout, or patient waiting.)
Verify the chatbot displays session expiration message — "Your session has expired. Please start a new chat to continue." (or equivalent).
Verify "Click to Start a New Chat" button is displayed.
Verify no partial booking changes were committed:
Check PNR "ST2H8K" directly in the booking system (Amadeus/Altea or test environment).
Confirm original flight AC200 is still active — NOT replaced by AC210.
Confirm no ticket reissue occurred.
Confirm no fare difference recorded.
Confirm no payment transaction initiated.
Click "Click to Start a New Chat."
Verify a completely new session begins:
Legal disclaimer displayed again.
No pre-filled PNR or surname.
No reference to previous session or flight selection.
Enter PNR "ST2H8K" and surname "Chen" again.
Complete OTP authentication again.
Verify eligibility re-evaluated from scratch.
Verify flight selection starts fresh — AC210 is not pre-selected or remembered.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that the chatbot session expires after the 2-hour timeout window, all unsaved progress is lost, and the customer must restart the flow from the beginning (disclaimer → authentication → flight selection → payment). No partial booking changes should be committed to the PNR. The original booking must remain completely unchanged.
 Preconditions: • Passenger: Laura Chen (ADT) • PNR: ST2H8K • Flight: AC200 YYZ → YVR — UNFLOWN, VOL eligible • Booking source: ACB, ticket stock: 014, Fare: Flex Economy • No blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, not checked in • Customer is mid-flow — has authenticated, selected alternate flight, reviewed fare difference, but has NOT yet confirmed the change or completed payment • Session has been active for 1 hour 59 minutes and is about to reach the 2-hour limit

PHASE 1 — CHATBOT ENTRY & AUTHENTICATION:
Given Laura Chen has Air Canada booking PNR ST2H8K for flight AC200 Toronto → Vancouver
And booking source is ACB, fare is Flex Economy
And no blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, not checked in
And Laura opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user says "I'd like to change my flight"
When the chatbot identifies booking change intent
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Laura accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Laura Chen"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Laura! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR ST2H8K"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR ST2H8K
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: l***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: l***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
ELIGIBILITY EVALUATION:
Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for PNR "ST2H8K"
And the system calls Eligibility Service
And the Eligibility Service evaluates Rules 1–8 → all PASS
And the booking is marked VOL Eligible
PHASE 2 — VOLUNTARY CHANGE FLOW — IN PROGRESS:
Then the chatbot displays:
"What kind of change are you looking for?"
• Change my flight
• Upgrade my flight
And Laura selects "Change my flight"
Then the system validates check-in status — not checked in, passes
And the system detects single passenger — bypasses multi-pax check
And chatbot displays:
"Let's look into some alternative flights together! What didn't work for you about your flight?"
• Date/Time
• Arrival/Departure Airport
And Laura selects "Date/Time"
Then chatbot displays date picker within ±7 day window
And Laura selects new travel date "24-Jun-2026"
And Flight Availability API returns alternate flights
And Laura selects flight AC210 with fare difference CAD $150
And chatbot displays itinerary comparison:
Old: AC200 YYZ → YVR on original date
New: AC210 YYZ → YVR on 24-Jun-2026
Fare difference: CAD $150
— AT THIS POINT: Laura has NOT clicked "Confirm" —
— AT THIS POINT: No payment link has been generated —
— AT THIS POINT: Session is at 1 hour 59 minutes —
— Laura steps away / gets distracted / leaves browser open —
PHASE 3 — SESSION TIMEOUT (2-HOUR MARK):
When the 2-hour session timeout is reached
And Laura has NOT clicked "Confirm" or completed any payment
Then the chatbot shall terminate the active session
And chatbot shall display session expiration message:
"Your session has expired. Please start a new chat to continue."
And a "Click to Start a New Chat" button shall be displayed
PHASE 4 — VERIFY NO PARTIAL CHANGES:
Then ALL unsaved progress shall be lost:
Authentication state cleared — Laura is no longer identified
Flight selection cleared — AC210 is NOT held or reserved
Fare calculation cleared — CAD $150 is NOT pending
No payment link was generated
No partial booking update was committed to the PNR
And the original booking shall remain UNCHANGED:
PNR "ST2H8K" still shows AC200 YYZ → YVR on original date
Original ticket coupon is unchanged — no reissue occurred
No fare difference recorded
No ancillary changes
PHASE 5 — RESTART FLOW:
When Laura clicks "Click to Start a New Chat"
And a new chatbot session begins
Then Laura must go through the full flow from scratch:
Legal disclaimer displayed again — Laura must click "I agree"
PNR entry required again — Laura must enter "ST2H8K"
Surname entry required again — Laura must enter "Chen"
OTP authentication required again
Eligibility re-evaluated
Flight selection starts fresh — previous selection (AC210) is not remembered
Fare calculation starts fresh
Payment required from scratch
And no reference to the previous expired session shall exist
And no pre-filled fields from the expired session shall appear
RESTART — FULL FLOW FROM SCRATCH:
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And Laura says "I'd like to change my flight"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer
And Laura accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (RESTART):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And Laura provides "Laura Chen"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Laura! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And Laura provides "PNR ST2H8K"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR ST2H8K
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND
AUTHENTICATION (RESTART):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity."
And Laura selects email for OTP
And Laura provides OTP code
And the chatbot validates OTP → CORRECT
And the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
And the voluntary change flow resumes from the beginning — no memory of expired session
And Laura must re-select flight, re-confirm, and re-pay from scratch

Expected Outcome:
Session expires at exactly 2 hours.
Session expiration message displayed clearly to the customer.
All unsaved progress is completely lost — authentication state, flight selection, fare calculation, everything.
No partial booking committed. Original booking (AC200 on original date) remains completely unchanged in the system.
No orphaned transactions — no pending payments, no held inventory, no partial ticket modifications.
Customer must restart the entire flow from scratch — disclaimer, authentication, OTP, eligibility, flight selection, payment.
New session has zero memory of the expired session.

⚙️ Payment link expiry handled gracefully — no system errors, no crashes.
🔢 Numbered steps
Launch the Air Canada chatbot.
Accept the legal disclaimer.
Enter PNR "PT9K5R" and surname "Clark."
Verify Authentication API retrieves booking successfully.
Complete OTP verification.
Verify eligibility passes — booking marked VOL Eligible.
Select "Change my flight."
Verify check-in validation passes.
Select change reason, date, and alternate flight AC310.
Verify fare difference CAD 200 displayed.
Verify itinerary comparison: AC300 (old) vs AC310 (new).
Confirm the change — click "Confirm."
Verify payment link is generated inside the active chatbot session.
Click the payment link — verify redirect to external payment page.
DO NOT complete payment. Leave the external payment page open without entering card details.
Wait until the payment link expires. (Note: Payment timeout window may differ from the 2-hour session timeout. Document the actual payment link expiry duration.)
Return to the chatbot.
Verify chatbot detects payment was not completed.
Verify payment timeout message is displayed — "Your payment session has expired. Your booking has not been changed." (or equivalent).
Verify no partial booking changes committed:
Check PNR "PT9K5R" directly in the booking system.
Confirm original flight AC300 is still active — NOT replaced by AC310.
Confirm no ticket reissue occurred.
Confirm no fare difference recorded in the PNR.
Confirm no payment charge processed on any credit card.
Confirm no EMD issued.
Verify inventory released:
Confirm AC310 is no longer held for Steven — seat/fare released back to general inventory.
Verify chatbot offers retry options:
"Try again" (re-generate payment link if flight still available)
"Select a different flight" (return to flight search)
"Cancel" (end flow)
Scenario A — Retry when flight still available:
Select "Try again."
Verify system checks AC310 availability.
If available: verify new payment link generated.
Complete payment this time.
Verify booking updated to AC310 and ticket reissued.
Scenario B — Retry when flight no longer available:
Select "Try again."
Verify system checks AC310 availability.
If NOT available: verify "flight no longer available" message displayed.
Verify chatbot offers to search for other flights.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate system behavior when a voluntary booking change payment link is generated within the chatbot session, the customer navigates to the external payment page but does NOT complete payment before the payment link expires. The system must detect the incomplete payment, release any held inventory, preserve the original booking unchanged, and offer the customer a path to retry or select a different flight. This is distinct from Session_Timeout_TC001 — in this case the customer has progressed further (confirmed the change and received a payment link) but the payment itself was not completed. 
Preconditions: • Passenger: Steven Clark (ADT) • PNR: PT9K5R • Original Flight: AC300 YUL → YVR — UNFLOWN, VOL eligible • Booking source: ACB, ticket stock: 014, Fare: Standard Economy • No blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, not checked in • Customer has completed the full voluntary change flow up to payment: authenticated, selected flight, confirmed change, payment link generated • Customer has clicked the payment link and navigated to the external payment page • Customer has NOT completed payment (card not entered, or payment abandoned)

PHASE 1 — CHATBOT ENTRY & AUTHENTICATION:
Given Steven Clark has Air Canada booking PNR PT9K5R for flight AC300 Montreal → Vancouver
And booking source is ACB, fare is Standard Economy
And no blocking SSRs, no checked baggage, not checked in
And Steven opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user says "I'd like to change my flight"
When the chatbot identifies booking change intent
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Steven accepts the disclaimer

Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Steven! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR PT9K5R"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR PT9K5R
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: s***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: s***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
ELIGIBILITY EVALUATION:
Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for PNR "PT9K5R"
And the system calls Eligibility Service
And the Eligibility Service evaluates Rules 1–8 → all PASS
And the booking is marked VOL Eligible
VOLUNTARY CHANGE FLOW — COMPLETE UP TO PAYMENT:
Then the chatbot displays:
"What kind of change are you looking for?"
• Change my flight
• Upgrade my flight
And Steven selects "Change my flight"
Then the system validates check-in status — not checked in, passes
And the system detects single passenger — bypasses multi-pax check

"Let's look into some alternative flights together! What didn't work for you about your flight?"
• Date/Time
• Arrival/Departure Airport

And Steven selects new travel date

Then the chatbot responds: "Got it! Here is a summary of requested change:"
And chatbot displays itinerary comparison:
Old: AC300 YUL → YVR
New: AC310 YUL → YVR
Fare difference: CAD $200
Then the chatbot responds: "Please note that there will be a charge of $200 for this flight change. Would you like to proceed?"
And Steven confirms "Yes"
PAYMENT LINK GENERATED:
Then the chatbot generates payment link inside the active session
And chatbot displays: "Please complete your payment using the link below."
And a payment link button is displayed
When Steven clicks the payment link
And Steven is redirected to the external payment page
And the external payment page loads successfully
— AT THIS POINT: Steven does NOT enter card details —
— AT THIS POINT: Steven leaves the payment page open —
— AT THIS POINT: Steven gets distracted / phone dies / walks away —
— AT THIS POINT: Payment link timeout countdown is running —
PHASE 2 — PAYMENT TIMEOUT:
When the payment link expires (timeout reached — payment window closes)
And Steven has NOT completed payment
And no payment transaction has been processed
PHASE 3 — CUSTOMER RETURNS TO CHATBOT:
When Steven returns to the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot shall detect that payment was not completed
And the chatbot shall display payment timeout message:
"Your payment session has expired. Your booking has not been changed."
PHASE 4 — INVENTORY & BOOKING VERIFICATION:
Then the selected flight AC310 shall be released back to inventory
(no seat or fare is being held for Steven)
And the original booking shall remain UNCHANGED:
PNR "PT9K5R" still shows AC300 YUL → YVR on original date
Original ticket coupon unchanged — no reissue occurred
No fare difference recorded
No partial PNR modification committed
And no payment transaction shall exist:
No charge to Steven's credit card
No pending payment record
No EMD issued
PHASE 5 — RETRY OPTIONS:
Then the chatbot shall offer options to the customer:
• Try again — generate a new payment link for the same flight AC310 (if still available in inventory)
• Select a different flight — return to flight search
• Cancel — end the flow without making any changes
SCENARIO A — CUSTOMER RETRIES PAYMENT (FLIGHT STILL AVAILABLE):
When Steven selects "Try again"
And the system checks if AC310 is still available
And AC310 is still available in inventory
Then a new payment link shall be generated
And chatbot displays: "Please complete your payment using the link below."
And a new payment link button is displayed
When Steven clicks the new payment link
And Steven is redirected to the external payment page
And Steven enters card details and completes payment of CAD $200 successfully
And Steven returns to the chatbot
Then the booking shall be updated: AC300 → AC310
And the ticket shall be reissued
And the chatbot responds: "Your ticket has been re-issued. You should receive an email confirmation shortly."
And the chatbot displays:
"Here's an overview of your new booking:
Flight: AC310
Route: Montreal (YUL) → Vancouver (YVR)
Date: [updated date]
A confirmation email has been sent to s***@gmail.com."
SCENARIO B — CUSTOMER RETRIES BUT FLIGHT NO LONGER AVAILABLE:
When Steven selects "Try again"
And the system checks if AC310 is still available
And AC310 is NO LONGER available (sold out during delay)
Then chatbot shall display:
"Unfortunately, the flight you selected is no longer available."
And chatbot shall offer to search for other flights:
"Would you like to search for a different flight?"
And Steven selects "Yes"
Then the chatbot returns to the alternate flight selection step
And Flight Availability API returns updated alternate flights (AC310 not included)
And Steven selects a new flight
And the standard fare comparison, confirmation, and payment flow resumes
SCENARIO C — CUSTOMER CANCELS:
When Steven selects "Cancel"
Then the chatbot shall terminate the voluntary change flow
And no booking changes shall be committed
And original booking AC300 shall remain unchanged
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything else I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."
That completes every test case in the Booking Change Involuntary test suite. Here's the full summary of all test cases covered:

Expected Outcome:
Payment link expiry handled gracefully — no system errors, no crashes.
No partial booking committed. Original booking (AC300) remains completely unchanged.
No payment processed. No charge to customer's credit card. No pending payment records.
Inventory released. Selected flight AC310 is released back to general availability — no seats or fares held indefinitely.
Customer is informed clearly that payment was not completed and booking was not changed.
Customer offered a retry path — can try paying again (if flight still available), select a different flight, or cancel.
If the flight becomes unavailable during the payment delay, customer is informed and offered alternatives.
Other — 4 test cases

Description: Validate involuntary disruption where passenger has SSR SPML/VGML and Air Canada has already rebooked the passenger.

Preconditions:
• Passenger: Sarah Johnson
• PNR: SJ4K8M
• Original flight AC700 YYZ → YVR = CANCELLED (UN)
• Rebooked flight AC710 YYZ → YVR = HK
• SSR: SPML / VGML
• Booking source: ACB
• Ticket stock: 014
• No checked baggage

Given Sarah Johnson has Air Canada booking PNR SJ4K8M for flight AC700 Toronto → Vancouver on 20-Jun-2026
And flight AC700 has been cancelled (segment status = UN)
And Air Canada has auto-rebooked Sarah on AC710 YYZ → YVR (segment status = HK)
And PNR contains SSR SPML/VGML (Vegetarian Meal)
And booking source is ACB, ticket stock 014
And no checked baggage exists
And Sarah opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And the user responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Sarah accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Sarah Johnson"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Sarah! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR SJ4K8M"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR SJ4K8M
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: s***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: s***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
JOURNEY SELECTION:
Then the chatbot retrieves booking details and displays:
"I found the following journey:
• Flight: AC 700
• Route: Toronto (YYZ) → Vancouver (YVR)
• Date: 20-Jun-2026
Is this the journey you'd like to check?"
And the user confirms "Yes"
And the system validates booking eligibility successfully
And BCINVOL eligibility returns ELIGIBLE
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you, I'm sorry to hear your journey hasn't gone according to plan. Let me help you with your flight disruption."
Then the chatbot checks if the passenger have a new rebooking → Yes
Then the chatbot responds: "Here's the new itinerary I have booked you on for this flight."
And new itinerary comparison is displayed with Old Flight card (AC700 — Cancelled) And New Flight card (AC710 — Confirmed)
And an alert information is displayed: "Please note that any Ancillary services that you had on your ticket may no longer be associated to your ticket. Any paid services that are no longer applied will be refunded."
Then the chatbot asks: "How would you like to proceed?"
And chatbot displays three options:
• Confirm proposed itinerary
• Select a new flight
• Cancel my booking
And Sarah selects "Confirm proposed itinerary"
SELF-SERVE CHECKS — SSR/MEAL/PET CHECK:
Then System performs Self-Serve Checks SSR checks shall be triggered
And system detects SPML/VGML SSR on the booking
And SPML/VGML shall block self-service
SPECIAL MEAL RESTRICTION:
Then the chatbot displays:
"I can help you initiate a booking change. To better assist you, we'll need to rebook this flight manually and re-append your add-on services. Let me transfer you to an agent who can help you. If you're already at the airport, please speak with an Air Canada representative."
And chatbot shall NOT proceed to ticket reissue
And Live Agent Handoff shall be triggered
And live agent handoff options shall be displayed
And flow terminates

Expected Outcome:
SELF-SERVICE BLOCKED.
Special meal SSR requires manual handling.
Passenger redirected to live agent support.

🔢 Numbered steps
Setup: Create ACV booking with 2 passengers (Anna + Brian White). Cancel flight AC300. Split PNR at airport — Brian moved to child PNR AV5K8N.
Launch chatbot as Brian.
Accept legal disclaimer.
Enter child PNR "AV5K8N" and surname "White."
Complete OTP verification.
Verify booking source on child PNR = ACV (retained from parent PNR AV5K8M).
Verify system identifies ACV as ineligible for self-service.
Verify ACV restriction message displayed with correct verbatim.
Verify "Contact Us | Travel Info & Support" clickable link displayed.
Verify NO self-service options shown — no change, no upgrade, no confirm, no search, no cancel.
Verify flow terminates.
📋 Raw Action / Description
Description: Validate that when an ACV (Air Canada Vacations) booking with 2 passengers is split at the airport (one passenger separated into a new child PNR), the child PNR retains the original ACV booking source and remains blocked from self-service. 
Preconditions: • Passengers: Anna White (ADT), Brian White (ADT) • Original PNR: AV5K8M (ACV booking source) • Original Flight: AC300 YYZ → YVR — CANCELLED (UN) • PNR split performed at airport — Brian moved to child PNR: AV5K8N • Brian's child PNR retains ACV booking source • Brian now contacts chatbot

PHASE 1 — SETUP (PRE-TEST):
Given passengers Anna White and Brian White had booking PNR "AV5K8M"
And booking source = ACV (Air Canada Vacations)
And original flight AC300 YYZ → YVR has been cancelled
And at the airport, PNR was split:
Parent PNR: AV5K8M — Anna White — ACV source retained
Child PNR: AV5K8N — Brian White — Source = ACV (retained from parent)
PHASE 2 — CHATBOT ENTRY:
When Brian opens the Air Canada chatbot
Then the chatbot displays greeting:
"Hello! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?"
Then user selects English
Then an alert information is displayed "Please note that by using this service, you will interact with an automated chatbot which uses AI to answer your questions and requests. Your personal information will be used to identify and authenticate you, to answer your request, and for other purposes detailed in the Privacy Notice."
Then chatbot asks "How can I help you today?"
And Brian responds: "My flight was cancelled"
And the chatbot displays legal disclaimer "Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision making process. To process your request, you'll need your booking reference and the full name associated to the booking. Depending on the changes you select, payment may be required. Please have access to your preferred method of payment. Would you like to get started?"
And Brian accepts the disclaimer
IDENTIFICATION (GenUC-01):
Then the chatbot responds:
"I can definitely help you with that. To start I'll just need to know some information to be able to process your request and identify you."
And the chatbot responds:
"Please provide your full name exactly as it appears on your ticket, including any middle names if applicable."
And the user provides "Brian White"
Then the chatbot responds:
"Thanks, Brian! And, what is your booking reference number?"
And the user provides "PNR AV5K8N"
Then the chatbot searches Trip Tracer for PNR AV5K8N
And Trip Tracer returns: PNR FOUND (Auth Flow 1)
AUTHENTICATION (GenUC-05):
Then the chatbot responds:
"To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll do this by sending a one-time verification code to either your email or phone number on file. Where would you like to receive it?"
And the chatbot displays options:
• Email: b***@gmail.com
• Phone: +1***567
And the user selects "Email: b***@gmail.com"
Then the chatbot responds:
"I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it."
And the user provides the OTP code "591837"
Then the chatbot validates the OTP code → CORRECT (attempt 1 of 5)
Then the chatbot responds:
"Your verification was successful. Let's proceed to the next step."
BOOKING SOURCE EVALUATION:
Then the system calls Order Retrieve API for child PNR "AV5K8N"
And the system evaluates booking source
And the system identifies booking source as ACV (source retained from parent PNR even after split)
ACV RESTRICTION — CHILD PNR BLOCKED:
Then the booking shall be marked INELIGIBLE for self-service (both VOL and INVOL)
And chatbot shall display ACV restriction message:
"For Air Canada Vacations-related requests, please contact our travel experts using the phone number listed on the Air Canada Vacations Contact Us page."
And a clickable link labeled "Contact Us | Travel Info & Support" shall be displayed
And self-service options shall NOT be displayed:
No "Change my flight"
No "Upgrade my flight"
No "Confirm proposed itinerary"
No "Select a new flight"
No "Cancel my booking"
And the flow shall terminate
END FLOW (GLOB-11):
Then the chatbot offers:
"Is there anything I can help you with?"
• I would like you to – new flow
• No
And the user selects "No"
Then the chatbot responds "Thank you for choosing Air Canada."

Expected Outcome:
Child PNR retains ACV booking source after split (per Pompa's confirmation in transcript).
Self-service correctly blocked — ACV restriction applies to split PNR.
ACV verbatim message and Contact Us link displayed.
No self-service change possible. Flow terminates.
